title: Senior User Researcher [Fixed Term]
description:
We’re looking for a Senior User Researcher to drive player insights and shape the future of our games. In this role, you will be the bridge between our audience and our development teams, ensuring that our creative and technical decisions are informed by a deep, data-backed understanding of the people who play our games.



Joining our Data & Insight team and reporting to the Head of Data & Insight, you will own the entire research lifecycle - from identifying key questions to delivering insights that resonate across the studio.

  


### **The Position**



This role is an opportunity to lead our player research strategy by combining high-level product thinking with hands-on execution. You will work to ensure that player voices are naturally integrated into our development process, helping us foster a culture where player-centric insights guide our path forward.

  

**What you'll do*** + Lead the research roadmap and strategy for our live games and upcoming titles.

	+ Design mixed-method studies, including surveys, playtests, and qualitative research to decode player motivations.

	+ Spearhead long-term initiatives such as player segmentation and feedback programs to track sentiment over time.

	+ Collaborate cross-functionally with UX, Design, Marketing, and Production to translate findings into actionable goals.

	+ Manage external partnerships and research vendors to ensure high-quality delivery.

	+ Synthesize complex data into clear narratives, using reports and workshops to guide stakeholders at all levels.
  

**Who you are*** + 5+ years of experience in player or UX research within the games industry.
	+ Skilled in mixed methods, with the ability to balance quantitative data with the qualitative "why."
	+ Proven track record of managing research projects end-to-end, from initial design to final delivery.
	+ Strong communicator who can turn data into insights that inspire and guide creative teams.
	+ Analytical mindset, with a genuine interest in player behavior and industry trends.
*Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.*

**The Stockholm location**
Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.

**Our values**
At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.

**How to apply**
To apply for this position, provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.

*At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.*
company_description: Avalanche Studios is the original creator of the award-winning Just Cause franchise (published by Square Enix) with the third entry release

title: CRO & Content Specialist – Ecommerce Europe
description:
At Fenix Outdoor, we create digital experiences that bring our outdoor brands to life. We are looking for a CRO & Content Specialist – Ecommerce Europe to optimize our online stores, improve customer journeys, and help people engage with our brands across Europe.


If you’re excited by this opportunity, apply now and start your next adventure with us!

**ABOUT THE ROLE**


As a CRO & Content Specialist – Ecommerce Europe at Fenix Outdoor, you will play a pivotal role in enhancing the customer experience and boosting conversionacross Fenix Outdoor’s portfolio of E-Commerce sites. You will work closely with our E-commerce Manager and Director, Merchandisers, and cross-functional teams in Marketing, Data Analytics, UX/UI, and Tech to identify opportunities, test hypotheses, and implement changes that improve engagement and online sales.


This position primarily focuses on Fjällräven’s European E-Commerce sites, with occasional support for other Fenix Outdoor brands such as Hanwag. You will combine your skills in conversion rate optimization, analytics, and CMS content management to ensure our digital storefronts deliver a premium, consistent brand experience while maximizing commercial performance.


This role is based in Solna, Sweden, reporting to the E-commerce Manager.


**WHAT YOU WILL DO**


In more detail you will focus on the following:

* Identify opportunities to improve conversion rate and user engagement through data analysis, user behavior insights, and experimentation
* Develop hypotheses and run A/B tests from setup to analysis and recommendations
* Optimize landing pages, product pages, sign-up flows, and key conversion points
* Manage and publish marketing and commercial web content across markets to drive engagement and conversion
* Maintain evergreen content such as legal and customer service pages
* Monitor and report on site performance, conversion, and engagement metrics
* Conduct performance reviews of homepage and content placements to provide actionable recommendations
* Collaborate with UX/UI, Tech, Marketing, and Data teams on optimization initiatives
* Participate in UAT, backlog prioritization, and requirements to improve site functionality and user journeys
* Act as a CMS expert and ensure consistent brand experience across all digital touchpoints

**WHO YOU ARE**


We think you could be a great match for the position if you possess the following traits and capabilities:

* 3-4 years of experience in conversion rate optimization (CRO) and content publishing
* Strong analytical mindset with hands-on experience using tools like GA4 to understand user behavior and identify optimization opportunities
* Proven experience developing hypotheses, running A/B tests, and translating results into actionable improvements
* Strong understanding of E-Commerce user journeys and optimization of landing pages, product pages, and key conversion flows
* Experience working with CMS platforms to implement and optimize on-site content
* Ability to turn data insights into clear recommendations that improve engagement, conversion, and online sales
* Comfortable collaborating with teams across E-commerce, Marketing, UX/UI, Data, and Tech
* A plus if you have experience with multi-brand or international E-Commerce sites, experimentation tools (like heatmaps or session recordings), or digital asset management systems such as Bynder

**HOW TO APPLY**


Submit your application in English via our application portal! Applications are processed on an ongoing basis and the position may be filled prior to the closing date.


This position is based in Solna, Sweden. Our office policy allows for one work-from-home day per week; on the remaining days we work from the office.


We look forward to reading your application!

title: Senior Fullstack Engineer — Monetisation Team (f/m/d)
description:
### **About us**


Pet Media Group (PMG) is a scale-up that acquires, launches, and develops market-leading pet marketplaces globally, with over 1.5 million animals finding new homes through our platforms every year. We currently operate in 6 countries (UK, Sweden, Netherlands, Italy, Spain, and most recently the US) — all within just 5 years of launching. Here's one of our apps: Pets4Homes.co.uk  

The way pets find new loving homes has remained largely unchanged for centuries. We're on a mission to change that through innovative technology — and increasingly, through the intelligent use of AI. We're a young and ambitious team who have built unicorns before (e.g. Avito), backed by some of Europe's leading investors (e.g. Verdane and Otiva — early backers of Klarna, CAIA, Apotea, and Easypark).

**Who are we looking for?**


We're expanding our Monetisation team and looking for an Senior Fullstack Engineer to help us build great products in the payment processing & value-added services space, and improve the user experience in the payment domain.


The Monetisation team works on several key parts of the business - in the VAS (value-added services) domain they build & maintain everything from pricing, checkouts/payment processing, recurring subscriptions, product activations, experimentation/CRO. They also build & maintain an escrow system that allows users to safely do the entire transaction through the platform, touching topics like KYC/KYB/identity verification, payment processing & payouts.  

  

We're not just looking for a strong engineer — we're looking for someone who actively uses AI tools as part of their daily workflow: whether that's using LLMs to accelerate development, exploring AI-powered tooling for testing, code review, or product ideation, or simply staying genuinely curious about where the technology is heading.  

We expect roughly 7 years of engineering experience, ideally with our current tech stack — and a mindset that sees AI as a multiplier, not a buzzword.

### **Requirements**


We're a fast-growing scale-up. We want engineers who build fast, fail fast, and learn fast. You don't need to be a world-class expert in every tool we use — but you do need a critical, open mindset, strong JavaScript proficiency, and a genuine enthusiasm for AI-assisted development.  

*Core (required):*

* Building apps with TypeScript, Next.js, Node.js & React
* Understanding of GraphQL and its usage in large systems with multiple consumers
* Experience with frontend/backend testing tools and testing strategies
* Experience with relational databases like PostgreSQL
* Experience with responsive design & mobile web UX
* Active use of AI tools (e.g. Claude, Cursor, Codex, ...) in your day-to-day engineering work — we want to hear how you use them

*Bonus (helpful & appreciated):*

* Experience with payment processing (Stripe, Adyen, Braintree)
* Experience with monitoring & observability tools like Grafana
* Experience with UI/component frameworks like Tailwind
* Experience evaluating or experimenting with AI coding workflows at team or company level


Working proficiency in English is required.

### **Our tech stack**


Next.js, React, Node.js & React Native (TypeScript throughout) · GitLab CI/CD · Elasticsearch · Redis · GraphQL & PostgreSQL · Knex · Docker · Serverless with AWS Lambda · AWS · Grafana · Bitrise

### **What we offer**

* Join a rapidly growing, already-profitable business with a clear vision and genuine energy
* A diverse team across Sweden, Germany, Austria, UK, Poland, Portugal, Bosnia, Croatia, Ukraine and Egypt
* A real opportunity to shape our tech department — and to help define how AI becomes part of how we build
* Competitive compensation and great benefits (remote or in-person)


We very much look forward to your application, contact us if you have any questions!

title: Senior Technical Project Manager
description:
**YOUR MISSION AT VOI**


As a Senior Technical Project Manager in the Vehicle Hardware Team at Voi, you will lead the procurement and development of next‑generation vehicles and components. From scooters and e‑bikes to batteries and other key hardware modules. You will typically own two parallel hardware procurement projects, steering them from early requirements and supplier selection through development, industrialisation, launch and post‑launch improvements. Reporting to the Head of Vehicle HW & QA, you will play a crucial role in ensuring our fleet is safe, reliable, repairable and efficient for our riders and markets.


You will:

* Lead end‑to‑end project management for typically two hardware procurement and development projects in parallel.
* Coordinate Voi teams and suppliers on timelines, milestones, risks, status and next steps to secure on‑time, high‑quality deliveries.
* Translate Voi’s needs into clear goals and technical requirements, and drive evaluation of samples across UX, repairability, reliability, energy efficiency and more.
* Support Voi engineers and suppliers on technical topics, contributing to documents such as DFMEA, PFMEA, FEA, Design for Reliability and Design for Repairability reports.
* Manage structured handling of actions, decisions, accountability and documentation to ensure smooth project progress.
* Present clear, concise project and technical status updates to the executive team, incorporating input from senior leadership into project plans.
* Build strong relationships and lead tough but constructive discussions and negotiations with suppliers on timelines, quality and technical issues.
* Travel regularly (e.g. quarterly) to China and occasionally to other supplier locations and Voi markets to work hands‑on with partners and in‑field issues.

**WHAT YOU NEED TO EMBARK**


You are a technically strong, hands‑on project manager who thrives at the intersection of engineering, suppliers, AI tools and business. You enjoy owning complex hardware projects end to end, can zoom between details and the big picture, and communicate confidently with both engineers and executives.


You have:

* A bachelor’s degree or higher in an engineering discipline (or equivalent practical experience).
* Strong experience working closely with hardware suppliers, ideally including Chinese partners.
* Proven experience collaborating with multi‑disciplinary hardware teams (mechanical, electrical, test & verification, certification) as well as procurement, firmware, QA and supply chain.
* Experience leading products through the full lifecycle – from goal setting and requirements through launch and resolving in‑field issues.
* Excellent presentation and communication skills, able to explain technical topics and business trade‑offs to senior stakeholders.
* Strong mechanical engineering understanding, including working with DFMEA, PFMEA, FEA, reliability test reports and hands‑on repairability assessments; CAD/CAE skills are a plus.
* Solid electrical engineering understanding to engage in DFMEA/PFMEA and review reliability test reports.
* Excellent analytical and technical problem‑solving skills; being data‑driven and comfortable with SQL is a plus.
* AI‑native working style and confidence using modern AI tools in your daily work to increase speed, structure and quality.
* Fluency in English, both written and spoken; proficiency in Chinese is a strong plus.
* The ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and to work effectively in a fast‑paced, scale‑up environment, collaborating across teams and time zones and travelling when needed.

*Don’t meet every requirement? If you are excited about this role and our mission, and have relevant, transferable skills, we encourage you to apply – even if you don’t check every box.*

**WHY VOI?**


Working at Voi is more than just a job; our People Promise includes a personal voyage where you will grow as a professional and be a part of a team and culture that builds something meaningful for society. In addition to this you’ll have the opportunity to:

* Join Europe’s #1 micromobility operator and one of the fastest growing scale‑ups.
* Get “skin in the game” through our employee options programme.
* Enjoy unlimited free Voi rides and a dog‑friendly office.
* Work together with inspiring, motivated and fun colleagues towards a common goal.
* Join the micromobility revolution and be a part of creating sustainable cities made for living, free from noise and pollution.

title: E-Commerce Content Writer
description:
**E-Commerce Content Writer**
-----------------------------


We are looking for an E-Commerce Content Writer to join our Nordic Knots team in Stockholm. We are searching for an experienced writer with a strong editorial eye and the ability to craft both inspiring brand storytelling and clear, conversion-driven product communication. You should be highly organised, detail-oriented and motivated by shaping how Nordic Knots is expressed across the digital shopping experience.

**The Role**
------------


As E-Commerce Content Writer, you will play a central role in developing content that strengthens our brand while improving the clarity and performance of our site experience. You will support the E-commerce and Marketing teams in scaling high-quality content production across product launches, category expansions and commercial campaigns.


This role combines structured content development with hands-on execution. You will contribute to improving how customers understand our products, navigate our assortment and engage with Nordic Knots across key digital touchpoints.


This role reports to the E-Commerce Director.

**Your Responsibilities**
-------------------------

* Write and optimise product descriptions, category texts and UX microcopy
* Develop campaign headlines, landing page content and merchandising narratives
* Support CRM communications including lifecycle emails, launches and key trading moments
* Collaborate closely with designers and marketing teams on campaign execution and content structure
* Ensure consistency in tone of voice and messaging across all digital customer touchpoints
* Support SEO content initiatives, structured content production and localisation workflows
* Contribute to improving content clarity, product education and conversion performance

**About You**
-------------

* Degree in Communications, Journalism, Marketing or a related field
* Minimum 4 years professional writing experience within e-commerce, retail or design-led consumer brands
* Fluent in English and Swedish, with excellent written communication skills in both languages
* Strong attention to detail and the ability to switch between brand storytelling and performance-focused writing
* Structured, proactive and comfortable working towards deadlines in a fast-moving environment
* Experience working with SEO content, localisation processes or content systems is a plus
* Interest in interiors, design or premium consumer brands is a plus

**Nordic Knots**
----------------


Nordic Knots was founded in 2016 by Liza Laserow, Fabian Berglund and Felix Berglund. We are a fast-growing direct-to-consumer Scandinavian interior design brand with the USA as the major market.


Nordic Knots was born from a culture of thoughtful design that lasts beyond seasons and trends. Fusing function and aesthetics with a Scandinavian sensibility, we craft timeless textiles for the modern home. We believe that great textiles are foundational and can transform a house into a harmonious home — framing the most beautifully curated lives.


The head office as well as the flagship store is located at Birger Jarlsgatan in central Stockholm. We have a constantly growing team in both Sweden and the USA.

**What We Offer**
-----------------


We are a driven team with high ambitions and an entrepreneurial, open-minded culture where new ideas and initiatives are encouraged. At Nordic Knots, everyone plays a meaningful role in contributing to our continued growth.


We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants who bring different perspectives, experiences and ideas to our team.

**Location**
------------


This position is based at our Stockholm headquarters.

title: Staff Embedded Software Engineer - Vehicle Access Systems
description:
About Us:

Rivian is on a mission to keep the world adventurous forever. This goes for the emissions-free Electric Adventure Vehicles we build, and the curious, courageous souls we seek to attract.  

As a company, we constantly challenge what’s possible, never simply accepting what has always been done. We reframe old problems, seek new solutions and operate comfortably in areas that are unknown. Our backgrounds are diverse, but our team shares a love of the outdoors and a desire to protect it for future generations.
Responsibilities:
* Lead the development of embedded systems for Vehicle Access with focus on security, reliability, and performance, owning the technical direction for digital key and access across multiple vehicles and programs.
* Design and implement system-level features within vehicle access areas like phone-as-key, defining and evolving the architecture, interfaces, and patterns used by multiple ECUs and services (vehicle, mobile, and cloud) for secure access.
* Lead cross‑functional technical work with security, mobile, cloud, UX, hardware, and manufacturing teams to balance security, usability, and serviceability for vehicle access.
* Develop and maintain embedded C/C++ (and optionally Rust) code for safety-critical applications, while guiding other engineers on design and implementation best practices.
* Work with UWB, NFC, networking, and security protocols to ensure secure and reliable vehicle access, owning fleet‑level reliability and mitigating systemic issues across markets and programs.
* Drive the technical roadmap and make key architectural decisions for the vehicle access domain, influencing how multiple teams design, implement, and integrate access‑related functionality.
* Ensure high quality through automated testing, code reviews, and CI/CD pipelines, and define quality, security, and testing standards adopted by multiple teams working on vehicle access.
* Mentor and support senior and junior engineers, fostering knowledge sharing and best practices, and acting as the go‑to technical reference for vehicle access.
* Operate independently and take ownership of complex, ambiguous, cross‑functional engineering challenges that span multiple systems and teams.

  


Qualifications:
* 10+ years of professional experience in embedded system development, including leading architecture and delivery of complex, cross‑team systems.
* Strong expertise in C/C++; Rust experience is a strong plus.
* Experience with security-critical systems, networking, and protocol development.
* Strong understanding of system-level development, vehicle security and related standards.
* Self-sufficient, highly independent, and able to take ownership of complex, cross-functional projects from early definition through launch, influencing stakeholders across teams and organizations.
* Excellent command of English, both written and spoken, for international collaboration.

**Nice to have**

* Experience in automotive access systems and security protocols.
* Previous experience leading technical teams in embedded development, including setting technical direction and standards at a domain or platform level.

  


Company Statements:
#### **Equal Opportunity**


Rivian is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practices laws. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender expression, gender identity, genetic information or characteristics, physical or mental disability, marital/domestic partner status, age, military/veteran status, medical condition, or any other characteristic protected by law.  

Rivian is committed to ensuring that our hiring process is accessible for persons with disabilities. If you have a disability or limitation, such as those covered by the Americans with Disabilities Act, that requires accommodations to assist you in the search and application process, please email us at candidateaccommodations@rivian.com.#### **Candidate Data Privacy**


Rivian may collect, use and disclose your personal information or personal data (within the meaning of the applicable data protection laws) when you apply for employment and/or participate in our recruitment processes (“Candidate Personal Data”). This data includes contact, demographic, communications, educational, professional, employment, social media/website, network/device, recruiting system usage/interaction, security and preference information. Rivian may use your Candidate Personal Data for the purposes of (i) tracking interactions with our recruiting system; (ii) carrying out, analyzing and improving our application and recruitment process, including assessing you and your application and conducting employment, background and reference checks; (iii) establishing an employment relationship or entering into an employment contract with you; (iv) complying with our legal, regulatory and corporate governance obligations; (v) recordkeeping; (vi) ensuring network and information security and preventing fraud; and (vii) as otherwise required or permitted by applicable law.  

Rivian may share your Candidate Personal Data with (i) internal personnel who have a need to know such information in order to perform their duties, including individuals on our People Team, Finance, Legal, and the team(s) with the position(s) for which you are applying; (ii) Rivian affiliates; and (iii) Rivian’s service providers, including providers of background checks, staffing services, and cloud services.  

Rivian may transfer or store internationally your Candidate Personal Data, including to or in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the European Union and in the cloud, and this data may be subject to the laws and accessible to the courts, law enforcement and national security authorities of such jurisdictions. **Please note that we are currently not accepting applications from third party application services.**

title: Marknadskoordinator
description:
**Vi växer och vill nu utöka marknadsteamet med en person som vill arbeta i en bred marknadsroll med stort ansvar, varierande arbetsuppgifter och högt tempo. Rollen innebär ett nära samarbete med många olika delar av organisationen.**

**Marknad på Blinto**
---------------------


På Blinto säljs entreprenadmaskiner, lastbilar och professionell utrustning på auktion till köpare över hela Europa. Varje vecka går hundratals objekt under klubban. Genom att ge nyttofordon och utrustning längre livslängd bidrar vi samtidigt till en mer hållbar värld.


Marknadsavdelningen är en viktig del av vår värdekedja och spelar en central roll i att skapa framgångsrika affärer.


Du kommer att ingå i ett marknadsteam med kollegor i Sverige och i våra övriga länder inom koncernen. Tillsammans driver ni den operativa marknadsrytmen för Blinto Sverige, men ibland stöttar ni även kollegor i andra länder. Vi arbetar som ett team.


Marknadsavdelningen i Sverige består idag av kommunikatörer, en UX/UI-designer och en marknadschef. Tillsammans ansvarar ni för att driva trafik till vår sajt och säkerställa att vår kommunikation håller rätt tonalitet och professionalitet.

**Om rollen**
-------------


I rollen ansvarar du för att projektleda och operativt driva både digitala och fysiska marknadsaktiviteter.


Ditt övergripande uppdrag är att öka kännedomen om Blinto och konvertera fler företagare till att köpa eller sälja sina verktyg och nyttofordon via oss.


I praktiken innebär det att du:

* prioriterar och planerar marknadsaktiviteter
* säkerställer att kampanjer går live i rätt tid
* stöttar säljorganisationen
* ser till att våra auktioner får den synlighet de förtjänar

### **Exempel på arbetsuppgifter**

**Event och mässor**  

Du koordinerar kundevent och mässor – från planering av bemanning till beställning av material och merch. När vi är ute på fältet säkerställer du att marknadsstödet fungerar hela vägen, från budskap till genomförande.

**CRM och kundkommunikation**  

Du ansvarar för nyhetsbrev, utskick och annan kundkommunikation. Målet är tydliga, relevanta och professionella budskap till våra kunder.

**Sociala medier**  

Du planerar och driver våra sociala kanaler genom att publicera träffsäkert innehåll, följa upp dialogen och säkerställa att kommunikationen håller rätt nivå och tonalitet.

**Uppföljning och analys**  

Du mäter och följer upp aktiviteter för att förstå vad som fungerar och vad som kan utvecklas. Det kan handla om KPI:er, kampanjresultat eller kundundersökningar.


Den här rollen ställer höga krav på struktur och projektledning i ett högt tempo. Du kommer ofta att fungera som spindeln i nätet och arbeta nära flera delar av organisationen, exempelvis säljledare, mäklare, techteam och kundservice.


Du hanterar både önskemål från organisationen och driver egna initiativ från idé till genomförande. Det kräver att du kan prioritera, kommunicera tydligt och se till att aktiviteter faktiskt blir genomförda.


Du kommer också att ha löpande kontakt med externa parter som tidningar, mässarrangörer och leverantörer av marknadsprodukter.

**Vem är du?**
--------------


Du är en person som får saker att hända – en klassisk doer.


Du trivs i ett högt tempo där många saker händer parallellt. När något blir liggande kliar det lite i fingrarna. Samtidigt har du en stark känsla för struktur, vilket gör att du inte bara startar saker utan också slutför och följer upp dem.


Eftersom du kommer att koordinera aktiviteter som involverar både interna kollegor och externa partners är du en tydlig och inkluderande kommunikatör. Du är social, samarbetsinriktad och har lätt för att arbeta med olika typer av människor.


Du är pragmatisk, prestigelös och lösningsorienterad. När något behöver göras ser du till att det blir gjort – och när något kan göras bättre driver du förbättringen.

**Vi tror att du har**
----------------------

* Eftergymnasial utbildning inom marknadsföring eller motsvarande
* Erfarenhet av operativ marknadsföring, growth eller marketing operations
* Erfarenhet av att sätta upp och hantera annonser i Meta Ads Manager (Facebook och Instagram)
* Vana att planera och driva leveranser i miljöer där många vill mycket samtidigt
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta nära sälj eller kommersiella team
* God förståelse för digital marknadsföring, analys och uppföljning
* Mycket god svenska och engelska i tal och skrift

**Extra meriterande**
---------------------

* Erfarenhet från marketplace- eller plattformsmiljö
* Kunskap inom CRM, segmentering och kundutskick
* Erfarenhet från bolag i stark tillväxt
* Öga för form samt förmåga att ta fram enklare grafiskt marknadsmaterial

**Om Blinto**
-------------


Blinto är en av Nordens största marknadsplatser för nätauktioner av nyttofordon och professionell utrustning. Vi är en del av Blinto Group, med ambitionen att bli en ledande aktör i norra Europa inom digital försäljning av återbrukade nyttofordon – och en viktig del av den cirkulära ekonomin.


Hos oss händer mycket. Beslutsvägarna är korta och arbetet ligger nära affären. Vill du vara med och driva marknadsarbetet i ett bolag där det du gör märks direkt, samtidigt som du utvecklas tillsammans med bolaget – då kommer du att trivas hos oss.

**Blinto erbjuder**
-------------------

* En heltidstjänst
* Placering på huvudkontoret i Växjö, med möjlighet till delvis distansarbete
* Konkurrenskraftiga villkor och förmåner
* En kultur präglad av stark gemenskap, med konferenser, aktiviteter och kunskapsdelning
* Ett bolag i stark tillväxt med högt engagemang och tydligt framtidsfokus

**Ansökan**  

För dessa tjänster använder vi oss av rekryteringsföretaget One Partner Group. Du slussas automatiskt till deras sida när du klickar dig vidare till ansök.

**Välkommen med din ansökan!**

title: Senior UX/UI-designer med AI fokus
description:
**Vill du jobba med framtidens design – tillsammans med AI?**
-------------------------------------------------------------


Design håller på att förändras i grunden och vi tycker att det är riktigt spännande. Med AI, nya designflöden och snabbare iterationer utvecklas rollen som UX Designer i rasande takt. Kanske har du redan börjat utforska AI-verktyg inom design som Pencil, Claude, Figma Make eller liknande? Eller så är du nyfiken på hur de kan förändra sättet vi jobbar med UX och visuell design framåt.


Hos oss får du möjlighet att vara med och forma det.


Älskar du UX och vet att det är grunden i all bra design? Är du dessutom visuellt stark och gillar att ta idéer hela vägen från insikt till färdig upplevelse?


Som **Senior UX/UI Designer på QueensLab** blir du en del av ett team där design och teknik möts på riktigt. Här handlar det inte bara om att rita snygga skisser, utan om att skapa verkligt värde genom smarta, genomtänkta och visuellt starka lösningar.


Vi ser att du har flera års erfarenhet i ryggen (gärna 8+ år) och är trygg i din roll som designer och trygg i att **driva designbeslut &** **kundleverans** framåt.

**Om rollen**
-------------


Du kommer att skapa och designa webb och digitala gränssnitt för stora som små kunder - men varje kund är lika viktigt. Det kan vara både som konsult ute hos någon kund eller som viktig del av någon leverans i våra inhouse kundprojekt, vi har ingen prestige utan kavlar upp armarna där vi behövs.


Men vi letar inte efter en “pixel pusher”. Vi söker dig som:

* Har en **stark visuell känsla** och kan skapa moderna, attraktiva och genomarbetade gränssnitt
* Förstår hela UX-resan, från **research och insikter till färdig design och implementation**
* Gillar att både **grotta ner dig i djup UX** och kunna jobba bredare när det behövs


Du säkerställer att våra leveranser håller toppklass genom:

* UX-research från användare och data
* Konceptutveckling och struktur
* Visuell design och interaktion

**Design + teknik = sant**
--------------------------


För oss är modern UX/UI Design inte isolerad från utveckling.


Vi ser gärna att du:

* Har positivt om du har erfarenhet av eller förståelse för **frontend/webbutveckling**
* Har jobbat nära utvecklare eller själv varit inne i kod
* Har erfarenhet av CMS som **Webflow, Umbraco, Shopify eller liknande**


Du behöver inte vara utvecklare, men possitivt om du förstår hur design blir verklighet.

**AI som superkraft (inte ersättare)**
--------------------------------------


Vi är ett AI-drivet bolag och vi förväntar oss att du också vill vara det.


Det innebär att du:

* Har ett intresse för hur **AI förändrar designyrket**
* Aktivt använder AI som verktyg i ditt arbete
* Förstår **när AI skapar värde, och när det inte gör det**


Hos oss är AI inte något som ersätter dig, det förstärker dig. Vi vill vara designers som skapar exponentiell effekt med AI som verktyg.

**Kultur & team**
-----------------


Vi tror på teamet som framgångsfaktor.


Hos oss:

* Har vi kul ihop och bygger starka relationer
* Delar vi kunskap och utvecklas tillsammans
* Testar vi nya idéer och utmanar varandra


Gillar du dessutom roliga upptåg och andra designkollegor så kommer du att trivas fin-fint hos oss.

**Vi erbjuder**
---------------


Kompetensutveckling
  

Generöst friskvårdsbidrag  

Flexibla arbetstider  

Jobbdator, mobilersättning och lurar  

Stans bästa läge vid Domkyrkan  

Pensionssparande  

Sjukförsäkring  

Trygghet och öppenhet mellan kollegor

**Låter det som du?**
---------------------


Bra. Då vill vi prata med dig.

**Vad väntar du på?**

title: Product Designer - Research focus
description:
Are you ready to redefine the financial industry? Are you looking to leave an impact on the financial lives and futures of over 2.35 million customers across the Nordics? Then Nordnet is the place for you!
   

  

**About Us**
 At Nordnet, we believe that groundbreaking products aren't born from assumptions, but from a deep, relentless curiosity about human behavior. Here, you’ll have the autonomy to challenge the status quo, the tools to uncover the "why" behind the data, and the influence to ensure that every pixel we ship is rooted in a real human need.
   

  

**Join us at Nordnet Product Design team**
 We aren't just looking for someone to run usability tests. We’re looking for a curious advocate who can help us understand the "why" behind the "what." You will be the bridge between our users’ lived experiences and our product roadmap, ensuring that every feature we ship solves a real human problem. Supported by your slides and research findings you’ll work side-by-side with designers and teams to ensure our pixels solve real problems. You are the 'voice of the user' who also knows how to roll a sleeve and translate research findings into tangible design solutions where necessary.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Lead the full research lifecycle, from scoping and recruiting to moderation, analysis, and reporting.
* Utilise a variety of qualitative and quantitative methods when conducting research.
* Actively present findings in compelling, creative ways that build empathy across the company.
* Work closely with Product Owners, Designers, Data Analysts and Engineers to turn raw data into actionable insights.
* Help represent the voice of the customer and influence the long-term product strategy.
* Translate research insights into low-to-high fidelity design concepts, wireframes, and prototypes to facilitate rapid iterative testing.
* Create a compelling presentation template & research hub for staff to use in their day to day work.
* Empower the wider design team by creating self-service research templates and coaching designers on best practices for testing.
* Partner with Data Analysts to triangulate qualitative 'why' with quantitative 'what' (behavioral analytics, funnel drops, and A/B test results).


**You are**
* A UX researcher with 5+ years of experience with a portfolio that shows how your work directly impacted a product.
* Not only handing over a research report and walk away. You sometimes stay in Figma file to ensure the solution actually honors the insight.
* Not only delivering data, yet as a great storyteller, through well crafted presentations you deliver narratives that make people care.
* Comfortable using different UX research methods and know when to go deep with a 1:1 interview and when a quick unmoderated test is enough.
* Comfortable with research tools and design tools like Figma or Maze.
* Comfortable challenging assumptions including your own.
* Active Participant in Design Reviews. You use your research lens to provide constructive feedback on team designs, helping colleagues spot potential usability hurdles before they even reach a testing phase.


**You have**
* Experience building or scaling research repositories.
* Experience navigating complex regulatory environments or designing for high-trust user journeys.
* Excellent communication skills and enjoyment of cross-functional collaboration.
* A keen eye for detail and a passion for high-quality, pixel-perfect design.
* A background in Cognitive Psychology, Anthropology, HCI, or a related field is a plus.


**Why you will love this role**
* You’ll be able to make a high impact in Nordnet Product experience.
* You’ll be able to affect collaboration across multiple teams within the organisation.
* You have an opportunity to shape how UX research scales in our organization.
* You’ll have room to innovate and establish new ways of working processes and challenge existing ones.


**About Nordnetters**
 We value experimentation and continuous improvement. With an impartial approach, we welcome new ideas to boost our progress. Sharing knowledge and learning from our setbacks is part of the game. We have the courage to ask questions and admit that we do not have the answer, all the time. We believe that straightforward and transparent communication build trust and inclusion.
   

  

 Culture is built and cared for, each day by everyone. We’re proud of ours. Having a flat organization where anyone can talk to anyone creates a warm and friendly atmosphere worth protecting. We believe in a culture where every effort counts and where everyone is being recognized. A culture embracing our core values – passion, simplicity and transparency on all levels, no matter who you are or what you do.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 We offer you the opportunity to work in a great team within a friendly Nordic environment with a strong focus on innovation, product development and technology. We are proud of the friendly and humble environment and commit to our goals as a team.
   

  

 We are 900 employees in Stockholm, Oslo, Helsinki and Copenhagen.
   

  

 You can also visit us on: www.nordnet.se, www.nordnet.no, www.nordnet.dk and www.nordnet.fi.
   

  

**Application**
 If you have questions, please contact Talent Acquisition Partner, Alva Hellberg, alva.hellberg@nordnet.se.
   

  

 Apply today– we will start interviewing immediately. Please note that we do only accept applications through our recruitment system, hence no email applications will be considered.
   

  

 We want to inform you that Nordnet conducts mandatory credit and background checks, as well as drug testing as a part of our recruitment process.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application!

title: UX Researcher - Mixed Methods
description:
**We’ve signed up to an ambitious journey. Join us!**
 At Arrive, we guide customers and communities towards brighter futures and more livable cities. Our people and our values are our strongest assets on this journey: We “Arrive Curious, Focused and Together”. Just as our entire brand is inspired by the North Star - the shining light leading travelers to their destinations since the beginning of time - our values serve us as guiding principles. They help us become our best: for our customers, and for the cities and communities we serve. As a global team, we are transforming urban mobility inspired by growing better, together, day after day.
   

  

**The Role**
 UX Research at Arrive focuses on fueling a customer-centric culture throughout product innovation and development. We seek to deeply understand the needs and experiences of customers and users. Through insights we empower product teams and leadership to make better decisions for design, development and strategy of innovative mobility solutions. We thus play a vital role towards our mission to ‘Together make cities more livable’.
   

  

 You’ll be part of a small and agile team that works across business units and product spaces. In particular, your work will focus on creating better experiences for cities and parking operators using our products to enable data-driven decision and policy making. You’ll also be responsible for leading measurement of product success and value across our family of products.UX Research at Arrive focuses on fueling a customer-centric culture throughout product innovation and development. We seek to deeply understand the needs and experiences of customers and users. Through insights we empower product teams and leadership to make better decisions for design, development and strategy of innovative mobility solutions. We thus play a vital role towards our mission to ‘Together make cities more livable’.
   

  

 You’ll be part of a small and agile team that works across business units and product spaces. In particular, your work will focus on creating better experiences for cities and parking operators using our products to enable data-driven decision and policy making. You’ll also be responsible for leading measurement of product success and value across our family of products.
   

  

**How To Make An Impact**
* Running qualitative research studies using a variety of methods to inform product direction and innovation
* Conducting quantitative research to assess the value of products for customers and users and triangulating findings with other data sources to influence decisions and strategies
* Partnering with designers and product managers in product discovery and validation to translate findings into ideas and actions
* Consulting with product teams to understand research needs and create strategic research plans
* Leveraging AI across the research lifecycle to accelerate our efforts and pioneering new ways of incorporating AI into UX research & strategy work


**About You**
* Comprehensive knowledge and experience using quantitative research methods (survey design & analysis, combination of perception and behavioral data)
* Solid experience in designing and conducting qualitative research studies using a variety of methods (such as interviews, diary studies, online usability tests, focus groups)
* Strong communication skills and the ability to present insights in an engaging way to product teams and product leadership
* Experience balancing multiple concurring research projects focused on creating impact
* Experience in storytelling and framework creation as means to communicate insights


**Your background**
* University degree in Human-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Anthropology, Sociology or related field
* Minimum of 5 years working as UX or user researcher
* Strong understanding of product development process and how research fits into the process


 Fluency in English is required for this position.
   

  

 This role is based in Stockholm, Lódz/Warsaw, London. We offer the opportunity for a hybrid office/remote setup.
   

  

 Arrive, including brands like EasyPark, Flowbird, RingGo, ParkMobile and Parkopedia, is a leading global mobility platform. Present in over 90 countries and 20,000 cities, the company helps people and decision-makers make smarter decisions about urban mobility and ease the experience of travel worldwide. Arrive delivers a unique combination of the core ingredients to make cities more livable: from smart payments and optimized car parks to data-driven traffic reduction and support for reinvestment in public transport and green space. It’s about more than function, it’s about saving time and simplifying the experience of travel for everyone. Travel is more than a journey, it’s how you Arrive.

title: Qualitative UX Researcher
description:
**We’ve signed up to an ambitious journey. Join us!**
 At Arrive, we guide customers and communities towards brighter futures and more livable cities. Our people and our values are our strongest assets on this journey: We “Arrive Curious, Focused and Together”. Just as our entire brand is inspired by the North Star - the shining light leading travelers to their destinations since the beginning of time - our values serve us as guiding principles. They help us become our best: for our customers, and for the cities and communities we serve. As a global team, we are transforming urban mobility inspired by growing better, together, day after day.
   

  

**The Role**
 UX Research at Arrive focuses on fueling a customer-centric culture throughout product innovation and development. We seek to deeply understand the needs and experiences of customers and users. Through insights we empower product teams and leadership to make better decisions for design, development and strategy of innovative mobility solutions. We thus play a vital role towards our mission to ‘Together make cities more livable’.
   

  

 You’ll be part of a small and agile team that works across business units and product spaces. In particular, your work will focus on our mobile apps that enable seamless parking experiences: from finding and reserving parking spaces, to managing access and payment in locations around the world. You’ll work with product teams and leadership to drive user-centricity and innovation in creating better parking experiences for drivers.
   

  

**How To Make An Impact**
* Conducting qualitative research using a variety of methods to inform product direction and innovation
* Triangulating research findings with other data sources from within the company to build holistic insights to influence decisions and strategies
* Partnering with designers, product managers and analysts to translate findings into ideas and actions
* Consulting with product teams to understand research needs, coach them in discovery and validation efforts and create tactical research roadmaps
* Leveraging AI across the research lifecycle to accelerate our efforts and pioneering new ways of incorporating AI into UX research & strategy work


**About You**
* You have robust experience in designing and conducting qualitative research studies using a variety of methods (such as interviews, diary studies, online usability tests, focus groups)
* You bring curiosity and team-spirit to any research challenge and task, taking stakeholders along the research journey and coaching them in their own efforts
* You excel at leveraging storytelling and have experience delivering insights in engaging ways to product teams, cross-functional partners and product leadership
* You know how to balance multiple concurring research projects focused on creating impact


**Your background**
 We don’t think one size fits all, but a successful candidate might have the following:
   

  

* University degree in Human-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Anthropology, Sociology or related field
* Minimum of 2 years working as UX, product or user researcher
* Strong understanding of product development processes and how research fits into the process
* Fluency in English is required for this position.


 This role is based in Stockholm, Lodz/Warsaw, London. We offer the opportunity for a hybrid setup
   

  

 Arrive, including brands like EasyPark, Flowbird, RingGo, ParkMobile and Parkopedia, is a leading global mobility platform.
