* Excellent communication skills in English, working confidently with global stakeholders


 Here at Sobi we live by our core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership!
   

  

**Additional Information**
***Please note:***
*As this role carries* 
*global responsibilities* 
*and can be based across Europe, it is published in several countries. You only need to submit* 
*one application* 
*— there is* 
*no need to apply separately* 
*to each posting.*

title: Production Engineer - Pharma
description:
At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.
   

  

**Your Role**
 We are looking for a Production Engineer for our pharmaceutical projects in Stockholm
   

  

*In this role you will play a key role in:*
* Operating independently within your area of expertise and lead others while driving competence development.
* Investigating deviations, support production technical issues, and contribute to improvement projects.
* Manage tasks such as issuing recipes and batch records, coordinating installations, or producing plant layouts and detailed designs.
* Lead or participate in subprojects related to process engineering, automation, or plant design.
* Ensure quality and documentation compliance according to given guidelines.


**Your Profile**
* Master’s or bachelor’s degree in science (or equivalent).
* At least 3 years of experience working in the pharmaceutical industry
* Process development and technology with experience from Process technology, Automation, Facility project planning
* Strong technical proficiency in Process Engineering, Automation, Plant Design, or Project Management.
* Ability to work autonomously and deliver results based on guidelines while assuring quality.
* Experience in leading small projects or subprojects and collaborating across functions such as plant engineers and design engineers.
* Good communication and problem-solving skills to support production and improvement initiatives.


**What We Believe You Will Enjoy About Working Here**
* We recognize the significance of flexible work arrangements to provide support. Be it remote work, or flexible work hours, you will get an environment to maintain healthy work life balance.
* At the heart of our mission is your career growth. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professions are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
* We support your personal development and offer an education library with more than 250 000 (!) courses and educations and certifications.


**Application**
 As part of our recruitment process, identity verification or background checks may occasionally be conducted.
   

  

 Valid work permit for Sweden is required.
   

  

 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

title: Lösningsarkitekt IT Beredskaps och säkerhetsapplikationer
description:
Svenska kraftnät är en nyckelaktör i energiomställningen. Vår IT-division växer för att möjliggöra en fördubblad elproduktion och en fossilfri framtid. Med ny teknik ökar vi både infrastrukturens kapacitet och utvecklingstakten inom digitalisering och processer.
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 I rollen som lösningsarkitekt kommer du formellt att tillhöra enheten Beredskap och Säkerhetsapplikationer på division IT, du kommer även att stötta andra enheter på avdelningen och arbeta brett inom olika verksamhetsområden. Den egna enheten ansvarar för att vidareutveckla och förvalta interna och externa applikationer och system, samt säkerställa att de uppfyller verksamhetens behov och krav.
   

  

 Som lösningsarkitekt har du en central roll i att brygga verksamhetens krav och önskemål mot IT och agera operativt, taktiskt och strategiskt för verksamhetens framdrift. Du kommer att hantera frågeställningar på olika nivåer samt översätta och förankra tekniska lösningar med fokus på hög tillgänglighet och IT-säkerhet inom applikationer, system och IT-infrastruktur.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att arbeta tätt tillsammans med erfarna kollegor som består av förvaltningsledare, systemansvariga, IT-specialister och systemutvecklare. Teamet ansvarar för applikationsdrift och support för ett flertal verksamhetskritiska applikationer och system, vilket omfattar såväl mjukvara som hårdvara.
   

  

**Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:**
* Samarbeta och medverka i projekt och förvaltningsorganisation
* Leda och driva framtagandet av lösningar utifrån såväl ett verksamhets- som tekniskt perspektiv
* Analysera, utveckla och föreslå förbättring kring nuvarande och framtida systemarkitektur och integration med fokus på säkerhet, prestanda och driftsäkerhet
* Säkerställa att lösningar följer lagkrav och etablerade standarder, riktlinjer och målarkitektur
* Medverka i informationsklassning, systemklassning samt risk- och sårbarhetsanalyser
* Visualisera och kommunicera komplexa tekniska lösningar för olika målgrupper, på olika nivåer i organisationen
* Bedöma konsekvenser av tekniska val och förändringar i IT-landskapet


 Hos oss får du möjlighet att arbeta i ett komplext systemlandskap med höga krav på säkra, robusta system med hög tillgänglighet inom en samhällskritisk verksamhet.
   

  

**Om dig**
**Skallkrav**
 Vi söker dig som är framåtlutad, analytisk och har förmåga att se helheten samt möjligheter i komplexa miljöer. Din framgång bygger på nyfikenhet och ett starkt relationsbyggande, vilket är en naturlig del av ditt arbetssätt. I rollen kommer du att möta flera delar av verksamheten och bidra till deras utveckling genom vägledning mot gemensamma mål. Du anpassar din kommunikationsstil efter målgrupp och kompetensnivå och kan förklara komplexa frågor på ett sätt som skapar förståelse, delaktighet och engagemang i hur lösningar ska utformas och realiseras. Du trivs med att arbeta tillsammans med kollegor för att leverera resultat med hög kvalitet. Samtidigt har du förmåga att se helheten och skapa strukturer som ligger i linje med övergripande målarkitektur och etablerade arbetssätt. Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Vidare har du utbildning från högskola eller universitet inom datasystemvetenskap, systemutveckling eller likvärdig utbildning eller kompetens förvärvad på annat sätt som Svenska kraftnät bedömer som likvärdig. Du har även erfarenhet av:
   

  

* Minst 5 års aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet i roll som lösningsarkitekt med erfarenhet av lösningsdesign av IT-infrastruktur, applikationer och IT-system.
* Minst 5 års aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av arbete med IT-miljöer baserade på VMWare, Microsoft Windows Server, Microsoft Active Directory.
* God förståelse kring IT-infrastruktur och nät.
* Erfarenhet av design av integrationslösningar och gränssnittsarkitektur (API, meddelandemönster).
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med kravhantering och att skapa spårbarhet mellan krav och design.
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska.


 Vi ser det som
 **meriterande** 
 om du har
   

  

* Erfarenhet av säkerhetsskyddad verksamhet och informationssäkerhetsarbete, involverande kritiska IT-system med hög skyddsklass.
* Erfarenhet av att designa och dokumentera arkitektur för isolerade IT-nätverk med hög säkerhetsklass (Röda Nät).
* Erfarenhet av offentlig verksamhet.
* Kunskap om arkitekturramverk, metodik samt erfarenhet av verktyg för arkitektur- och modellering (t.ex. Sparx EA, Archi).
* Erfarenhet från agila arbetssätt och verktyg


**Bra att veta**
* Tjänsten är placerad i Sundbyberg
* Möjlighet till distansarbete upp till 50 %
* Sista ansökningsdag 2026-04-08. Vi använder urvalsfrågor istället för personligt brev och önskar ditt CV skrivet på svenska.
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning
* Förmåner | Svenska kraftnät


 I denna rekrytering samarbetar Svenska kraftnät med Computer Sweden Recruitment. Vid frågor, kontakta rekryteringskonsult Johanna Nordberg, johanna.nordberg@csrecruitment.se, 070-33 43 159 alternativt Victoria Waltenberg, rekryterare hos Svenska kraftnät. Fackliga representanter är Annika Ingeborn, SACO, 010 475 87 72 och Erica Midfjäll ST, 010 475 87 31. Du når oss också via e-post: fornamn.efternamn@svk.se
   

  

 Jobba här – bli en viktig del av vår energiframtid | Svenska kraftnät
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare i samband med rekrytering, vi har upphandlade avtal som gäller för Svenska kraftnät som statlig myndighet.
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) att genomföras i de fall befattningen innebär hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerade uppgifter eller deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet. Om anställningen avser befattning i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövningen även att innefatta en registerkontroll och, i vissa fall, en särskild personutredning. För anställning på Svenska kraftnät kan det vara ett krav på svenskt medborgarskap i vissa befattningar. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. För mer information om säkerhetsprövning Svenska kraftnät arbetar för att främja mångfald och motverka diskriminering och ser gärna sökanden med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter till våra rekryteringar. Lär känna Svenska kraftnät och hur det är att arbeta hos oss: Lär känna oss på Svenska kraftnät

title: Office Manager
location: Upplands Väsby och Sollentuna, Stockholm County, Sweden
description:
**Want to shape the future of waterborne transport and join a world-class team?**
 Candela is the
 **global leader in electric hydrofoil vessels** 
 . Since 2014, we’ve gone from building our first premium electric speedboat in a small Stockholm workshop to delivering game-changing vessels around the globe.
 **The Candela P-12** 
 is our latest innovation - the world’s first electric hydrofoil ferry is not just operating in Stockholm, but now attracting orders from around the world.
   

  

 Our boats “fly” thanks to
 **computer-guided** 
 underwater wings under the hull made out of
 **lightweight carbon fiber.** 
 The wings lift the vessel above the water, which results in cutting energy use by up to 80%, while offering zero emissions and a far better passenger experience than traditional diesel vessels.
   

  

 And that’s exactly why we do this. Our mission is clear: to
 **speed up the transition to fossil fuel–free lakes and oceans** 
 .
   

  

 Are you a proactive, organized, and people-oriented professional who loves keeping things running smoothly? Candelas is looking for an
 **Office Manager** 
 to take the lead in managing our operations at our Rotebro factory, with occasional work at our smaller Frihamnen office. This is a hands-on role where no two days are the same – from coordinating office operations to supporting new hires and helping shape a vibrant workplace culture.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
**Run the Show at Rotebro**
* Take ownership of the day-to-day operations at our Rotebro factory, making sure everything is organized, efficient, and welcoming
* Build and maintain routines and processes to ensure safety, structure, and smooth operations.
* Handle office purchases, supplies, equipment, and all operational needs.
* Manage access for employees and visitors, including coordinating access cards.
* Liaise with vendors and service partners to keep everything running smoothly.
* Monitor and coordinate maintenance and repair for the office and facilities.
* Oversee office space planning, seating arrangements and office moves.
* Spend most of your time on-site in Rotebro, with occasional visits to Frihamnen.


**Support Our Team**
* Coordinate onboarding for new employees and make sure their workspaces, equipment, and access are ready from day one.
* Work closely with IT to provide hardware and support.
* Work closely with HSE manager to help coordinate activities such as health checks for employees and hands on support to production staff.
* Manage distribution of workwear and other essentials.


**Create a Great Workplace Culture**
* Organize team events, office activities, and company celebrations.
* Be a key player in fostering a collaborative, inclusive, and positive work environment.


**Support Leadership (10–20%)**
* Provide practical and administrative support to the CEO/Founder.
* Assist with coordination, logistics, and ad hoc tasks for the leadership team.


**Who You Are**
* Highly organized and proactive, with an eye for detail
* Flexible and adaptable, able to handle a variety of tasks
* A great communicator who thrives on collaboration
* Service-minded and positive, with a “can-do” attitude
* Comfortable working in a dynamic, fast-paced environment
* Holder of a valid driver’s license
* Fluent in English and Swedish


**Working Hours**
* Monday–Friday, 08:00–17:00


**Form of employment** 
 : Full-time with initial 6 months probation period
   

  

**Start date:** 
 ASAP
   

  

**Location/base** 
 : Rotebro (sometimes in Frihamnen)
   

  

**Team** 
 : HR
   

  

**Reporting to** 
 : Chief Human Resources Officer
   

  

**Why Join Us?**
 At Candela, you’ll be part of an international team that values collaboration, initiative, and a positive work culture. You’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact on how our offices and factory operate, and be a key part of our growth journey.
   

  

**We welcome the opportunity to meet you!**

title: Cloud Architect - Azure & AI
description:
*Vill du vara med och forma framtidens molnarkitektur och AI-plattformar i en dynamisk och växande affärsmiljö? Hos oss får du en central roll där tekniskt ledarskap, innovation och samarbete står i fokus. Välkommen till ett kompetent och engagerat team, där du får möjlighet att påverka, utvecklas och skapa verkligt affärsvärde!*
**Om jobbet**
 Som Platform Arkitekt i vårt Public Cloud Platform-team får du ett avgörande ansvar för att modernisera och operationalisera vår Azure molnarkitektur, teknikstrategi och styrning genom tydligt tekniskt ledarskap. Du har en central roll i vår satsning på AI och digital innovation och bidrar till att skapa en framtidssäkrad plattform som möter verksamhetens behov. Du samarbetar nära med kollegor inom Cloud Center of Excellence och är med och driver utvecklingen av vår tekniska miljö. I rollen får du möjlighet att påverka, utvecklas och skapa affärsvärde i en dynamisk och växande organisation där samarbete och ständig utveckling står i centrum.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer vara bland annat:
   

  

* Bidra med expertis till projekt och stötta verksamheten med molndesign och integrationsmönster
* Utveckla och etablera en modern teknisk plattform för AI och digital innovation
* Ge tekniskt ledarskap och mentorstöd till kollegor
* Dokumentera och optimera molnarkitektur enligt ramverk (bland annat Microsoft CAF, SAF och WAF)
* Hålla dig uppdaterad om nya teknologier och driva förbättringar inom plattformen
* Presentera och leda workshops samt kommunicera teknik och komplexa samband till olika målgrupper
* Samarbeta nära med teamet för att säkerställa leverans och utveckling mot verksamhetens mål


 Följande kommer göra dig framgångsrik i denna tjänst:
   

  

* Djup expertis inom molnarkitektur på Azure (gärna fler hyperscalers)
* Erfarenhet av ramverk som Microsoft CAF, SAF och WAF
* Microsoft-certifiering, gärna Azure Solutions Architect Expert, AI Fundamentals och/eller AI Engineer
* Förmåga att agera som teknisk ledare, mentor och arkitekt med fokus på design och optimering av molnbaserade AI-lösningar
* God kunskap om moderna AI-teknologier (ML Ops, NLP, GenAI) och Responsible AI, inklusive AI-etik, bias och explainability
* Stark erfarenhet av infrastruktur som kod (Terraform, Bicep, ARM)
* God förståelse för CI/CD och DevOps-praktiker
* Mycket god kommunikationsförmåga på svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift


 Meriterande är även om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av att bygga förmågor inom Agentic AI och Agentic Coding för att automatisera komplexa arbetsflöden
* Praktisk erfarenhet av containerlösningar som Docker, Kubernetes och OpenShift
* Bakgrund inom systemutveckling eller digital produktutveckling, gärna från bank- eller försäkringsbranschen


 Som person har du förmåga att arbeta under ständig förändring och trivs med att ta initiativ och ansvar. Du är självgående, planerar och coachar teamet mot gemensamma mål och har mod att misslyckas – och att lära dig av det. Med din starka kommunikationsförmåga engagerar du både chefer och andra arkitekter, och din positiva och engagerade attityd bidrar till ett öppet och utvecklande klimat där samarbete står i centrum.
   

  

**Kontaktuppgifter:**
 Rekryterande chef: klaus.seidel@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Forena: jessica.loow@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen: lisa-maria.carensjo@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

*Låter det spännande? Då vill vi veta mer om dig! Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag. Vi arbetar löpande med denna rekrytering och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.*
 Länsförsäkringar AB är en inkluderande arbetsplats där mod, möjligheter och engagemang står i centrum. Vi erbjuder ett hållbart arbetsliv med flexibla villkor och satsar på att alla medarbetare ska kunna växa och trivas, oavsett bakgrund.
   

  

 Digital & IT är hjärtat i Länsförsäkringsgruppens digitala utveckling. Vi driver den gemensamma digitala transformationen och levererar förstklassiga IT-lösningar, datadrivna insikter och innovativa digitala tjänster som skapar värde för hela verksamheten. Vårt uppdrag är att bygga en robust och framtidssäker IT-miljö – både på plats och i molnet – som möjliggör kundnära och effektiva affärer i hela gruppen.
   

  

 Hos oss möts teknik, affär och innovation. Vi arbetar tvärfunktionellt ute i verksamheten i agila team där utveckling, drift och strategi går hand i hand. Vi ansvarar för allt från IT-styrning, enterprisearkitektur och leverantörshantering till applikationsutveckling, cybersäkerhet och verksamhetsberedskap.

title: Student Assistant - Account Receivables & Collection
description:
**Working at Mercell**
 At Mercell, we're on a mission to revolutionize public procurement, making it accessible and effortless for everyone involved. Imagine a world where public buyers and visionary suppliers come together seamlessly to make a great deal easier and shape the future. That's what we're all about.
   

  

 With a dynamic culture built on continuous growth, trust, and collaboration, Mercell offers an environment where your talents can thrive. You will be part of an international environment with ambitious and dedicated colleagues who are passionate about what they do and supported to be themselves.
   

  

 Together, let's shape the future and create real value for society, one innovative solution at a time.
   

  

**Your Mission**
 This is an opportunity to contribute to the financial operations of Mercell while gaining valuable hands-on experience within finance and customer operations.
   

  

 As a Student Assistant in Accounts Receivable & Collections at Mercell, you will be part of the Finance team and report to the Billing & Collections Manager. Our team is responsible for ensuring accurate invoicing, timely payments, and professional handling of customer inquiries related to billing and outstanding balances. We support the wider organization by maintaining healthy cash flow and a positive customer experience in all financial interactions.
   

  

 As our new student assistant, you will play an important supporting role by helping manage incoming tickets related to debt collection, assisting with customer communication, and ensuring cases are handled efficiently and documented correctly. Your work will help the team maintain high service quality while enabling our specialists to focus on more complex cases.
   

  

 We see the following qualities as important to thrive and evolve in this role:
   

  

* Service-minded, accountable, and thorough, with a positive and professional attitude when interacting with customers and colleagues.
* Self-starter who can work independently and make productive use of quieter periods.
* Able to stay calm and organized under pressure, managing multiple tickets and deadlines effectively.
* Curious and quick learner, eager to understand systems, processes, and the basics of debt collection.


**Core Responsibilities**
* Review and respond to incoming support tickets related to debt collection inquiries, using templates and internal guidelines.
* Verify account and invoice information in internal systems to ensure accurate responses to customers and partners.
* Escalate complex or sensitive cases (e.g., disputes, legal matters, or large balances) to senior team members.
* Document actions and communication in the ticketing system to maintain clear case history.
* Assist with follow-ups and basic administrative tasks, such as sending reminders or collecting supporting documentation.


**Required Qualifications**
* Currently enrolled as a student in Finance, Accounting or a related bachelor's or master's degree.
* Experience in billing, debt collecting, accounting or order-to-cash processes will be a plus.
* Good experience navigating through different IT systems.
* High attention to detail and ability to manage multiple requests.
* Fluent in Swedish and English.


**Start date:** 
 As soon as possible
   

  

**Duration:** 
 Temporary part time (expected around 25 hours per week) until 31.08.2026
   

  

**Workplace type:** 
 Hybrid
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm (Sweden)
   

  

**Application Deadline: 31.03.2026.** 
 However, do not wait to submit your application - we will read applications and talk to potential candidates as we receive them
   

  

**What We Offer**
 As a fast growing technology company, we are committed to taking care of our employees through initiatives such as hybrid work to support work-life balance, health and insurance plans (may differ per country/office), pension plans, paid parental leave, social happenings and competitive salary packages. Please see https://career.mercell.com/posts/our-perks-benefits to get more insights on what to expect of perks, benefits and culture when joining Mercell.
   

  

**Has this sparked your interest?**
 Then we can't wait to have you join our mission and looking forward to receiving your application!
   

  

* Mercell is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.
* Mercell handles all personal data in line with GDPR and our Privacy Policy.
* Mercell does not provide relocation support. We are seeking candidates who are already based in, or have existing work authorization for, the country where the role is located.
* Mercell may carry out background checks on applicants to verify information that appears on CVs and other documentation. This background check is carried out by an external party and is not carried out without the consent of the applicant. Current applicants will receive further information about this.

title: Senior IT Projektledare
description:
**Företagsbeskrivning**
 AFRY erbjuder tjänster inom teknik, design, digitalisering och rådgivning. Vi är dedikerade experter inom industri, energi och infrastruktur och skapar värde för kommande generationer. AFRY har nordiska rötter med global räckvidd. Våra gemensamma principer – Result driven, Client centric, Empowering och Accountable – vägleder oss i hur vi arbetar, utvecklas och lyckas tillsammans. Var med och accelerera övergången till ett hållbart samhälle.
   

  

**Jobbeskrivning**
 Vi söker en erfaren projektledare för uppdrag inom ett flertal branscher och med stort fokus på offentlig sektor, energi och försvar. Arbetet bygger på ett stort intresse för människor, teknik och bryggan där emellan. Konsultrollen innebär i mångt och mycket att vara en rådgivande part gentemot våra kunder och bygga förtroende.
   

  

 I detta uppdrag hjälper du våra kunder med digitalisering genom att driva och leda utvecklings- och implementationsprojekt. Detta i olika typer av digitaliseringsinitiativ, både som förvaltningsledare och projektledare. Du etablerar förtroende och kommunicerar över hela organisationer på operativ till strategisk nivå.
   

  

**Kvalifikationer**
* Erfarenhet som huvudprojektledare med budgetansvar inom IT, systemutveckling eller digitalisering.
* Bakgrund inom offentlig sektor, energi- samt försvarsindustrin.
* Akademisk examen inom systemutveckling, management eller liknande.
* Certifiering i projektledning och kunskap om förvaltningsstyrning (PM3, ITIL eller motsvarande).
* Flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift.


**Meriterande:**
* Erfarenhet av agil utveckling, konsultarbete och kravarbete i förstudiefas.
* Kunskap om LOU och erfarenhet inom energi och förvarsmakten.


 Vi lägger stor vikt på dina personliga egenskaper. Du är en relationsbyggande projektledare och konsult som säkerställer bra samarbete och förståelse för kunds behov. Du hanterar stakeholder management väl och etablerar förtroende. Vidare är du affärsinriktad och nätverkande, ser potentiellt mervärde för kund och lyfter förslag genom tydlig kommunikation och presentation.
   

  

**Ytterligare information**
 Som anställd hos oss kommer du att ingå i ett team med härliga kollegor där våra kärnvärden genomsyrar vårt dagliga arbete. För oss innebär det en gemenskap och ett driv att utmana oss själva och våra kunder i att utvecklas och bli starkare. Vårt team består av hjälpsamma och omtänksamma kollegor där vi värderar gemenskap och balans mellan arbete och fritid.
   

  

 Gruppen består idag av 25 konsulter med erfarenhet som sträcker sig från några år till uppemot 25 år, i olika roller och inom flera sektorer. Vår gemensamma drivkraft är viljan att driva och koordinera för att stötta våra kunder utifrån olika utmaningar samt möjligheter.
   

  

**Varför jobba med mig? – Fredrik, rekryterande chef:** 
 Som ledare är jag närvarande, stöttande och möjliggör framgång genom att ta bort hinder. Jag drivs av att prestera tillsammans och imponeras dagligen av vilken potential som finns hos varje medarbetare i gruppen. Mitt främsta mål är att vi som grupp ska leverera den bästa projektledningen till våra kunder samtidigt som vi har grymt kul på jobbet, vilket bygger på att alla tar ansvar för både leverans och teamwork.
   

  

 Hos oss får du möjligheten att ta del av:
   

  

* Möjlighet till hybridarbete om uppdrag/kund inte kräver annat
* Individuell kompetensutvecklingsplan inom ditt spetsområde
* En AFRY Buddy som bollplank under din första tid som anställd
* Tillgång till stort utbud aktiviteter genom vår sociala klubb: Club AFRY, till exempel yoga, restaurangbesök, språkkurser med mera
* Konkurrenskraftiga förmåner gällande pension, friskvård och föräldraledighet reglerade i vårt kollektivavtal
* Företagsrabatter med externa partners genom vår förmånsportal Advantage You
* Ett enormt nätverk av tekniska experter med + 18 000 kollegor med kontor i 50 och projekt i 100 länder


**Välkommen med din ansökan, sista ansökningsdag är 2026-04-20**
**Kontaktperson för frågor:**
 Fredrik Lindblad, Business Section Manager
   

  

 fredrik.f.lindblad@afry.com
   

  

 För samtliga rekryteringar på AFRY genomförs som grund alltid en ID-kontroll, referenstagning samt verifiering av utbildning.
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.
   

  

*På AFRY driver vi förändring i allt vi gör. Vi anser att förändring sker när modiga idéer möts, när vi samarbetar, skapar innovation och omfamnar kreativa lösningar, det är så vi skapar framtiden. Vi söker konstant kvalificerade kandidater som vill ansluta sig till våra inkluderande team runt om i världen. Bli en del av oss och påskynda den gröna omställningen*

title: Product Developer Denim - H&M Divided
description:
**Job Description**

*We’re looking for a Product Developer with a strategic mindset, a strong passion for denim and fashion, and a collaborative way of working to join our Divided Denim team at H&M Womenswear.*




*This is a 1-year temporary position with placement in Stockholm, Sweden.*




  

  





**WHAT YOU’LL DO​**
  

 In this role as a Product Developer for H&M Divided Denim, you will play an important part in shaping a modern and relevant denim assortment for our global customer. You will define and execute both short- and long-term strategies for your denim category, ensuring strong alignment with our overall assortment goals. With your fashion insight, commercial thinking and deep understanding of denim trends, fabrics, fits and washes, you will drive seasonal keys, identify emerging opportunities and translate insights into desirable and well-crafted denim pieces. You will continuously refine our denim product and component strategy to support growth and profitability.
   

  





 You will take ownership of moving initiatives forward in a fast-paced environment, ensuring seamless execution and effective collaboration across the team. You will work closely with a product manager, planner and designer, and you will collaborate cross functionally across the business. You will also work directly with production teams and suppliers throughout the development process to secure fit, quality, sustainability and expression, which are all key pillars within denim.
   

  





 With your passion for denim and product development, you will be essential in creating world class pieces that are on trend, expressive and truly resonate with H&M Divided customers.
   

  





  

  





**WHO YOU ARE​**
  

 To succeed in this role, we believe you have:
   

  




* Five years of experience in a similar role as Product Developer or Buyer
* Education in textiles, buying or product development
* Proven track record in creating denim products for the womenswear customer, with a strong sense of fashion and refined taste
* Excellent insight into current fashion trends and the ability to translate them into desirable and commercially relevant denim pieces
* Deep passion for product and product development, with a strong interest in denim design, construction, fits, fabrics and washes
* Extensive material knowledge related to denim, including cotton qualities, blends, weave types, treatments, finishes and wash techniques
* Driven, structured and proactive in taking initiative
* Commercial mindset with a results oriented approach
* Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross functional teams
* Strong ability to identify opportunities, improvements and efficient solutions across the denim development process



  

  





**WHO WE ARE​**
  

 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
   

  





 ​
   

  





**JOIN US​**
  

 Additional information
   

  

 This is a
 **1 year temporary position, starting in Spring 2026, with placement in Stockholm.** 
 If you feel your experience, skills and ambitions are right for this role, please send your application including CV as soon as possible. Interviews will be held continuously. (For internal applicants, please remember to initiate a dialogue with your manager before applying).
   

  





 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​
   

  





 Benefits:
   

  

 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer all our employees at H&M Group attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET. In addition to our staff discount, all our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program – HIP.
   

  





 Inclusion & Diversity:
   

  

 H&M is a part of H&M Group. At H&M Group, we’re determined to create and maintain inclusive, diverse and equitable workplaces throughout our organization. Our teams should consist of a variety of people that share and combine their knowledge, experience and ideas. Having a diverse workforce leads to a positive impact on how we address challenges, on what we perceive possible and on how we choose to relate to our colleagues and customers all over the world. Hence all diversity dimensions are taken into consideration in our recruitment process.
   

  





 We strive to have a fair and equal process and therefore kindly ask you not to attach a cover letter in your application as they often contain information that easily can trigger unintentional biases.
   

  





  

  





**Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.**

title: MS Fabric / Power BI Engineer
description:
**Role Overview**
 We are looking for a Senior Microsoft Fabric / Power BI Engineer to support a large-scale enterprise environment within the banking sector. The role focuses on platform enablement, technical configuration, access management, and continuous improvement of Power BI and Microsoft Fabric environments, ensuring stability, scalability, and compliance.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Enable and optimize Microsoft Fabric within the client environment
* Manage and configure Power BI platform (access management, governance, configurations)
* Ensure stability and performance of business-critical reporting services
* Implement system improvements, automation, and platform enhancements
* Oversee platform lifecycle activities (patching, upgrades, feature installations)
* Ensure compliance with Change Management processes for deployments
* Manage licensing compliance and proper usage of platform components
* Provide technical guidance and support to internal users and stakeholders
* Deliver knowledge transfer to internal teams


**Required Skills & Experience**
* Strong hands-on experience with Power BI and Microsoft Fabric
* Solid expertise in platform configuration, access management, and governance
* Experience with enterprise BI environments and large-scale data platforms
* Good understanding of IT processes (Change Management, Release Management)
* Experience in system lifecycle management (patching, upgrades, configurations)
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
* Fluent in English


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience in the banking or financial services sector
* Experience with automation and platform optimization
* Power BI report development experience


**Engagement Details**
* Start Date: April 7, 2026
* End Date: December 31, 2026
* Location: Stockholm
* Work Setup: Hybrid (3 days/week on-site)
* Allocation: Full-time (100%)
* Contract Type: Freelance / B2B

title: Front Office Ambassador At Six - Full time
description:
At Six is more than a hotel.
   

  

 It’s a house for cosmopolitan minds, where art, culture and contemporary luxury meet in the heart of Stockholm.
   

  

 Now, we’re looking for a Front Office Ambassador, who lives for exceptional service, personal connections and unforgettable first and last impressions. Someone who understands that true luxury is not about formality, but about attention, intuition and presence.
   

  

 You’ll be a key part of our Front Office team, setting the tone for the At Six experience and ensuring every guest feels seen, welcomed and taken care of — from arrival to departure, and everything in between.
   

  

**About Us**
 Part of Petter Stordalen’s Nordic Hotels & Resorts, At Six is a contemporary art and design hotel with 343 rooms and suites, including our Masterpiece Suite and 32 long-stay apartments. In the house, you’ll also find Dining Room, Blanche & Hierta, At Six Cocktail Bar, a gym, wellness facilities and 3000 m² of flexible meeting and event space.
   

  

 Since opening in 2017, At Six has been redefining luxury in a contemporary context. Today, we are one of Scandinavia’s leading choices for high-end travelers thanks to our people, our standards, and our ambition to always deliver more than expected.
   

  

 The role
   

  

 As Front Office Ambassador, you are at the heart of the guest experience. You are the main point of contact for our guests, ensuring personalised service, smooth operations and memorable moments throughout their stay.
   

  

 This is a hands-on, guest-facing role for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys problem-solving and takes pride in delivering service on an international level. You’ll work closely with Front Office, Reservations and other hotel departments to ensure seamless communication, swift solutions and consistently high guest satisfaction.
   

  

 Your mission?
   

  

 To elevate every stay, and to help make At Six the benchmark for service in Stockholm and beyond.
   

  

 Key responsibilities
   

  

* Guest Experience: Welcome guests, manage requests, handle feedback and ensure a warm, personalized experience throughout the stay.
* Service Excellence: Actively resolve challenges, anticipate guest needs and deliver the At Six wow factor, even in demanding situations.
* Coordination & Communication: Serve as the link between guests and hotel departments, ensuring smooth collaboration and flawless execution.
* Front Office Operations: Support daily Front Office activities, including check-in, check-out and guest follow-ups.


 Who you are
   

  

 You have a genuine passion for hospitality and understand that luxury lives in the details. You are confident, calm under pressure and enjoy being the person guests turn to when they need something, or when they don’t even know they need it yet.
   

  

**You Bring**
* At least 1 years of experience within Front Office or Reservations
* Experience working in the luxury or premium hospitality segment
* Strong knowledge of a Mews PMS System
* Fluency in Swedish and English
* A flexible mindset and availability to work varied shifts, including weekends and holidays
* A positive attitude, strong social skills and a true team spirit
* A proactive, solution-oriented approach you enjoy challenges and take initiative
* A valid driver’s license is a MUST


 Ready to join At Six?
   

  

 If you thrive in a dynamic, international environment and are motivated by creating memorable guest experiences, we would love to meet you. At Six offers a supportive and inspiring workplace in the heart of Stockholm, where high standards meet warmth, collaboration and long-term growth.
   

  

 Some of our benefits
   

  

* Four complimentary hotel nights per year at any Strawberry hotel
* Employee and Friends & Family room rates
* Discounts on hotel stays, food and beverages across the group
* A vibrant workplace with opportunities to grow — professionally and personally


 Interviews are held on an ongoing basis and the position may be filled before the final application date.

title: Talent Acquisition Specialist
description:
**Talent Acquisition Specialist – Partnering with Managers Globally & Driving Employer Branding Initiatives**
 Talent Acquisition plays a critical role in shaping both our organisation and our culture. As we continue to grow and scale globally, attracting the right people in the right way is essential to our long-term success.
   

  

 We are now looking for a Talent Acquisition Specialist to join our Global HR team, based in Stockholm. In this role, you will take full ownership of end-to-end recruitment processes and act as a close partner to hiring managers across the business. You will also play an active role in employer branding initiatives, helping to create compelling content and strengthen our visibility in key talent markets.
   

  

**Role**
 Our business operates in a regulated industry, with high complexity, where we recruit complex specialist roles across functions, seniorities, and geographies – including the key areas below:
   

  

* Commercial – including both regional and central functions, such as field sales and marketing positions operating in complex, regulated, and highly specialised markets (B2B2C).
* Product development – including QA, RA, Clinical Affairs, and R&D positions supporting regulatory approval.
* Manufacturing / Operations – including Process Engineers, Production Project Managers, and Supply Chain roles, responsible for our manufacturing sites, ensuring operational excellence and compliance.
* Corporate – including Finance, Legal, HR, Office, and other central corporate functions supporting our global operations.


**Team & Culture**
 You play a key role in building the organisation and culture we want to grow with. Recruitment is not just about filling roles – it is about long-term alignment, trust, and bringing people into an environment where they can succeed and make an impact.
   

  

 You will join a collaborative Global HR team that values pragmatism, transparency, and a business-first mindset. You will report to the Talent Acquisition Manager and work closely with our Talent Acquisition Specialist, HR Partners (internal title: Talent Enablement Partners), and the wider HR team to enable growth, capability-building, and scientific excellence.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**End-to-end recruitment & stakeholder partnership**
* Lead full recruitment processes from start to finish, in close collaboration with hiring managers.
* Act as a trusted advisor, supporting managers in driving structured, high-quality, and scalable recruitment processes.
* Ensure a strong candidate experience while balancing speed, quality, and business needs.


**Sourcing, attraction & screening**
* Drive proactive and strategic sourcing for both high-volume roles and complex specialist roles with high requirements.
* Manage and prioritize screening activities in environments with large candidate inflows while maintaining quality and consistency.
* Use data, market insights, and recruitment analytics to guide decisions and continuously improve outcomes.


**Employer Branding & content**
* Work with Employer Branding initiatives across multiple channels, including career site, blog posts, company magazine, LinkedIn and other digital platforms.
* Create and adapt content in collaboration with HR, Marketing, and other important stakeholders.
* Contribute to strengthening our employer value proposition and visibility in relevant talent markets.


**Continuous improvement**
* Contribute to the ongoing development of a more dedicated and scalable global Talent Acquisition setup, including ways of working, tools, and processes.
* Apply and promote evidence-based and structured recruitment methodologies, with a business-oriented mindset, across the recruitment lifecycle.
* Participate in continuous improvement initiatives and selected HR and TA-related projects in collaboration with the global HR team and the business.


**Skills and requirements**
**Required Qualifications**
*Years of experience is not a strong predictor for future performance; however, to set you up for success and meet our current needs, this role requires certain skills, experience, and knowledge from day one – while still offering plenty of room to grow and develop further.*
* Experience driving end-to-end recruitment processes in a scale-up or growth environment, working closely with hiring managers and key stakeholders. Comfortable owning the full recruitment lifecycle independently and ensuring high-quality outcomes.
* Recruiting complex specialist roles across different functions, seniorities, and geographies.
* Applying structured and evidence-based recruitment and assessment methodologies, such as structured interviews (e.g., STAR), psychometric assessments, or case assessments, as part of the selection process.
* Using ATS and digital recruitment tools, leveraging data and insights to guide decisions and continuous improvement.
* Experience in employer branding initiatives, such as creating content for career sites, blogs, LinkedIn, and candidate communications.


**Language Requirements**
* Fluency in English is required, along with fluency in either Swedish, German, Spanish, Italian, Polish, or Chinese (Mandarin/Cantonese), as you will be working in a global environment.


**Beneficial Qualifications**
* Broader employer branding experience, including creating digital content (e.g., videos, photography, graphics) and/or using data to measure and enhance the impact of employer branding initiatives.
* Industry- or function-specific experience – including managing recruitment within the key areas outlined in the introduction: Commercial, Product Development, Manufacturing / Operations, or Corporate.


**Personality**
*What drives us, how we communicate, and how we respond when things don’t go as planned are key to our success. That’s why we focus on the traits and behaviours that help people thrive – and as a Talent Acquisition Specialist, you’ll both embody these qualities and help identify them in others.*
* Ambitious & Accountable – You are goal-oriented, take ownership of your commitments, and stay focused even in challenging situations.
* Collaborative & Influential – You build trust, foster strong relationships, and effectively influence hiring managers and other stakeholders.
* Adaptable & Growth-Minded – You are curious, see opportunities, learn from experiences, and approach change with a positive mindset.


**Other Information**
 This position is based at our central HQ in Stockholm, Klarabergsviadukten 63. We have a hybrid work model, primarily working from the office with flexibility to work from home.
   

  

**Recruitment process**
 The process will include interviews, psychometric assessments, a case assessment, and reference checks. You will meet with different colleagues and stakeholders to gain a thorough understanding of the role and Nordberg Medical, ensuring a mutual fit for both parties. We look forward to getting to know you better!
   

  

**Why should you join our team?**
* Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of a modern, global Talent Acquisition function.
* Continuous personal and professional development by leading recruitment for key roles across various functions, seniorities, and geographies.
* Join a dynamic, growing organisation where high-quality recruitment truly makes a difference.
* Work with dedicated, collaborative, and friendly colleagues across the globe, and see the tangible impact of your work.


**What is it like to work in Talent Acquisition at Nordberg Medical?**
 To give you a better sense of the role and our culture, we asked Yuting (Carmen) Zhu, one of our Talent Acquisition Specialists, to share her experience.
   

  

**Which roles and teams do you collaborate with most, and what types of requisitions are you handling?**
 I work closely both within the HR team and cross-functionally, especially with teams such as Quality Assurance, R&D, Regulatory Affairs, Clinical Affairs, and Marketing.
   

  

 At the moment, I mainly support roles like QA Production Specialists and Global Event Manager, and I also partner on HR-related roles such as an HR Operations Manager. It’s a nice mix of technical and commercial hiring, which keeps my work both challenging and interesting.
   

  

**What do you enjoy most about working at Nordberg Medical, and what keeps you motivated?**
 What I really enjoy is the combination of global exposure and a strong company culture. In my role, I support hiring managers across different countries, which means I’m constantly learning about new job functions, cultural backgrounds, and ways of working.
   

  

 At the same time, Nordberg Medical has a very transparent and inclusive culture. Our voices are genuinely heard, and even small contributions are recognised and appreciated. That creates a positive working environment and keeps me motivated and engaged every day.

title: Implementation Specialist - Germany
description:
****About Intric****
 Intric is accelerating its growth, backed by new funding and strong demand from both existing and new customers.
   

  

 Our platform, Intric.ai, is transforming how work gets done – particularly within the public sector and among organizations that prioritize data sovereignty. We make advanced AI accessible, secure, and practical. Today, we hold a market-leading position within our niche, and we are continuing to scale across Europe.
   

  

 We are now looking for an Implementation Specialist who wants to be part of building something meaningful from the ground up.
   

  

****About the Role****
 As an Implementation Specialist, you play a critical role in ensuring our customers succeed with Intric. You are the bridge between our platform and the organizations that rely on it.
   

  

 Your focus is not only on understanding customer needs, but on translating those needs into real, value-creating implementations. You educate, guide, and inspire users to adopt AI in ways that meaningfully improve their daily work.
   

  

 This is a hands-on role where you work closely with customers, internal teams, and subject-matter experts to ensure smooth onboarding, strong engagement, and long-term success.
   

  

****What You’ll Do****
* Lead customer onboarding and implementation projects from start to finish
* Educate and inspire users on how to leverage the platform effectively
* Analyze customer workflows and identify opportunities where AI can create efficiency and impact
* Facilitate seamless integration of Intric into day-to-day operations
* Collaborate with customer stakeholders and internal product teams to ensure successful outcomes
* Drive engagement and long-term adoption by building strong, trust-based relationships


****Who You Are****
 You are someone who enjoys working closely with people while understanding systems and processes at a deeper level.
   

  

 You:
   

  

* Communicate complex ideas clearly and persuasively
* Have a service-oriented and pedagogical mindset
* Learn new technology quickly and feel comfortable navigating digital tools
* Are curious about how organizations operate and enjoy understanding workflows
* Thrive in collaborative environments and work well with experts across different domains
* Take ownership and proactively drive projects forward


****Must-Haves****
* Strong communication skills in English & German, both written and verbal
* Experience working with customers in a consultative or implementation-focused role
* Ability to understand and map business processes
* High level of structure, ownership, and follow-through
* Genuine interest in AI and how technology can improve real-world operations


****Why Join Intric****
* Rapid career development in a fast-moving sector
* Competitive compensation package including salary and stock options
* Ownership and influence: bring your ideas to life
* Be part of a visionary team shaping the future of AI in Europe


****Practical Information****
 Location: Germany, primarily remote with occasional travel to the HQ or customers
   

  

 Travel: Regular travel within Germany and occasional cross-market collaboration
   

  

 Languages: German and English
   

  

**Benefits**
* Competitive salary and stock options
* 25 days of paid vacation
* Wellness allowance
* Rapid career development in a fast-growing AI company


****Culture at Intric****
 At Intric, we are building secure AI solutions for organizations that truly matter. We work with governments and enterprises where quality, responsibility, and trust are non-negotiable.
   

  

 We value ownership, high standards, and thoughtful execution. Titles matter less than contribution. If you see something that can be improved, you take initiative. If something is unclear, you ask questions. If a customer faces a challenge, you lean in.
   

  

 We combine ambition with collaboration. We move fast, but we care deeply about doing things right. As we continue scaling across Europe, every team member plays a meaningful role in shaping both the company and the culture.

title: Talent Acquisition Partner
description:
*Nordnet is a leading pan-Nordic digital platform for savings and investments. Ever since our start in 1996, our purpose has been to democratize savings and investments. Through passion, simplicity, and transparency, we challenge traditional ways in the financial industry, and give private savers access to the same information, tools and services as professional investors. Our ambition is to inspire, encourage and enable people to take their savings to the next level, no matter who they are.*
*Are you an experienced talent acquisition professional with a passion for building teams in close collaboration with your hiring managers? Do you want to be part of a winning, happy, and dedicated Talent Acquisition team? Then join us in the journey of shaping the future of Nordnet!*
**We believe this is you**
 As a Talent Acquisition Partner, you will independently manage recruitments for all organizational levels, aligned with hiring plans and replacements. The main part of your work consists of daily collaboration with hiring managers while conducting targeted candidate sourcing through LinkedIn Recruiter and other platforms to build strong talent pipelines. Acting as a proactive talent partner, you are responsible for recruitment, sourcing, selection and assessment and you will own end-to-end hiring activities specializing in recruiting a diverse range of professionals, from tech-heavy roles to asset management, marketing and administrative positions.
   

  

 Beyond recruitment, you will contribute to developing Talent Acquisition processes, improving tools, and running employer branding activities to attract and engage top talent. With a focus on creating an excellent candidate experience, you will directly connect with potential hires, inspiring them to join the organization.
   

  

 You will be part of a passionate and dedicated team with a humble, "can-do" attitude. You work for a flexible employer with short decision paths where you as an individual can be involved and influence to a great extent. A lot happens on a daily basis, so you thrive best in a dynamic environment. You take pride in and a sense of accomplishment in closing those hard-to-fill types of roles.
   

  

**Your background**
 We are seeking a passionate, hands-on recruiter with a curious mind and a humble attitude. You have previously documented experience from recruitment from or towards IT-heavy companies in agile environments. You have a well-developed network in the industry.
   

  

 You bring 4+ years of full-cycle recruitment experience across diverse business functions. As a data-driven professional with a 'digital-first' mindset, you are a proactive problem-solver with a sharp commercial sense and a passion for driving results.
   

  

**To be successful in this role, we see that you have experience of:**
* High growth environment
* Operating as the expert in industry best practices for all functional areas of recruitment, monitoring new trends and opportunities to consistently raise the standard for recruitment at Nordnet
* Create requirements profiles, understand business needs and lead/coach recruiting stakeholders through the recruitment process
* Proactively sourcing, building awareness of the very best passive candidates and creating a "talent bench" for future hiring
* Managing the recruitment process from initial candidate contact to offer, ensuring an outstanding candidate experience
* Execute tactical sourcing strategies through various channels and activities
* Working to promote our culture of diversity and inclusion both internally and externally
* Professional fluency in both written and spoken English


**What We Offer**
 We offer you the opportunity to work in a great team within a friendly Nordic environment with a strong focus on innovation, product development and technology. We are proud of the friendly and humble environment and commit to our goals as a team.
   

  

 We offer a hybrid working model, but we encourage an office first approach.
   

  

 We are 900 employees in Stockholm, Oslo, Helsinki and Copenhagen.
   

  

 More info about Nordnet can be found on our company website and in our "brand movie" This is Nordnet, which describes our identity.
   

  

 You can also visit us on: www.nordnet.se, www.nordnet.no, www.nordnet.dk, www.nordnet.fi and www.nordnet.de.
   

  

**Application**
 If you have questions, please contact Head of Talent Acquisition, Heidi Ershult, heidi.ershult@nordnet.se.
   

  

 Please note that we do only accept applications through our recruitment system, hence no email applications will be considered.
   

  

 We want to inform you that Nordnet conducts mandatory credit and background checks, as well as drug testing as a part of our recruitment process.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Lead Systems Programmer
description:
As the Lead Systems Programmer, you will play a key role in shaping and evolving the core systems that drive our games technical foundation. This is a hands-on leadership role, balancing individual technical contribution with coaching and developing a team of engineers.
   

  

 You will lead a team of programmers who are responsible for evolving our engine and its core systems such as spawning, memory management, job scheduling, threading frameworks, streaming, TRCs, while profiling, debugging, and optimizing across PC and current-generation consoles. Your work will influence both the project and shared engine technology across the studio in both the short and longer term vision of the project. You will exercise autonomy within the technical direction of the project.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
**Technical Leadership**
* Own and evolve core runtime systems, ensuring stability, performance, and scalability.
* Make day-to-day systems-level decisions in collaboration with the Technical Director and Technical Art Director.
* Drive technical standards, code quality, and best practices across the team.
* Profile, debug, and optimize the game on PC and consoles.
* Contribute improvements to shared systems to benefit the wider studio.


**Team Leadership & Career Growth**
* Mentor and support your direct reports, fostering professional growth. As their manager you are responsible for the entire employee life cycle and everything in between.
* Encourage knowledge sharing and collaboration within the team.
* Promote a culture of technical excellence and continuous learning.
* Provide constructive feedback and career guidance.
* Ensure the team thrives within the project’s technical direction.


**Collaboration & Delivery**
* Work closely with producers and other leads to maintain smooth project execution.
* Remove blockers and support high team productivity.
* Advocate for technical excellence across disciplines.
* Align your team with broader project goals.
* Facilitate effective cross-team communication.


**Who You Are**
* Experienced professional programmer in the gaming industry.
* Strong C++ and low-level systems expertise.
* Experience working with games engines in shipped titles or a live project.
* Experience working with proprietary engines is a plus.
* At least one current generation console title shipped.
* Experience with Console platform SDKs.
* Leadership experience with a focus on mentoring technical teams.
* Deep understanding of console architectures and performance constraints.
* Strong communicator and collaborative team player.
* Commitment to code quality, testing, and robust systems.


 Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.
   

  

**The Stockholm location**
 Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.
   

  

**Our values**
 At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
   

  

 At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.
   

  

 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Project Planner
description:
* About this opportunity


 Professional Scheduler / Project Planner will provide oversight of establishing initial project baseline through completion, acting as owner's representative.
   

  

 Kindly provide your CV in English.
   

  

**Responsibilities include but are not limited to:**
* Facilitating the development of a detailed project schedule along with project team and contractor input.
* Developing and improving status collection process to ensure quality progress reporting.
* Analysing project schedules including comparing cost/resource curves to actual, tracking float consumption, and change order impact analysis to ensure contractor is compliant with scheduling specification.
* Validating schedule updates along with a narrative for submission to the project team and stakeholders
* Identifying and mitigating threats to the project’s schedule goals and initiate corrective actions through field staff as necessary
* Interfacing with site management regarding critical schedule activities, change, impacts to schedule, change orders, project progress, and claims analysis.
* Providing final review of schedules for estimating as requested.
* Facilitating the team in revising logic for schedule compression, forensic analysis to identify cause and effect of delay and reporting.
* Analysing resource loading and adjust the schedule or initiate corrective action as appropriate.
* Providing information as needed on the content, modification, or presentation of project work with master scheduling to assess impact(s) of design/construction changes and schedule slippages, as well as to show the hierarchy of plans and schedules, and keep the project team fully informed.
* Reporting on impact analysis for potential delays and changes to project team.
* Managing change orders, requests for information and updates to project scheduling specific documents.
* Interpreting project team needs; assessing requirements and identifying solutions to non-standard requests with regards to project schedule
* Advising directors promptly of any issues that have arisen, or may arise, on projects; in particular those that might impact on Gleeds professional indemnity (PI) insurance.


**As a Gleeds team member, you will have access to:**
* Opportunities to develop and grow your career
* Employee Assistance Programme


* Who we’re looking for:


**Experience, Knowledge And Key Skills**
* 10+ years of scheduling / project control experience working on data centres construction projects
* Expert knowledge of MS Project
* Direct experience in data center construction a plus.
* Excellent communication skills with both internal and external stakeholders
* Sound ICT skills, with a high proficiency in MS Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
* Ability to work as part of a team.
* Fluency in English.
* Eligible to work in Sweden.


* A world of opportunity


 Gleeds is a place to be part of something bigger. We take pride in our diverse and supportive culture that empowers you to make a difference while creating connections with brilliant people – colleagues and clients alike. Enjoy doing work that shapes our collective future, while steering your own journey in a trusting environment. Benefit from our rich heritage and global opportunities as you construct a career with purpose.
   

  

 With over 75 offices across the globe, you’ll become part of a truly global team that isn’t restricted by borders. We welcome new perspectives and ideas but above all, we believe that quality relationships sit at the heart of good business. We strive to be the people that seek to understand and bring people together. Wherever we are in the world, we’re committed to the success of our people, clients and communities – giving everyone a safer and more sustainable place to live, work and thrive.
   

  

 Our values underpin what we stand for and how we work:
   

  

* Professionalism with personality
* Excellence with humility
* Innovation with agility


**We’re an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications regardless of age, disability, marital status, race, religion or belief, sexual orientation, transgender status or gender.**
**We recognise the importance of work/life balance and agree flexible working arrangements to suit each individual.**
**Gleeds is a Great Place to Work certified employer.**

title: Account Executive, LE/GE, GTS
description:
**About Gartner:**
 Are you looking for a fast-paced career with outstanding advancement potential at a global market leader? If so, think about Gartner. The world's leading research and advisory company, Gartner (NYSE:IT) delivers the technology-related insight necessary for our clients to make the right business decisions, every day. We are seeking highly motivated, achievement-driven sales associates to contribute to our double-digit growth, backed by the solid infrastructure of a world-class sales organization. In addition to a competitive base salary, uncapped commissions and exceptional benefits, Gartner sales associates can enjoy generous performance-based rewards, including all expenses paid trips to exotic locations around the world. Come be a part of our incredible growth organization!
   

  

**Position Summary:**
 The Account Executive is a field-based, direct sales role responsible for both client retention as well as growth through contract expansion and the introduction of new products and services. You will consult with C-level executives to develop and implement an effective, enterprise-wide strategy that maximizes the value delivered by Gartner’s products and services.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Account management with an outcome of increased customer satisfaction and an increase in retention and account growth
* Mastery and consistent execution of Gartner’s sales methodology
* Account planning and territory management
* Managing forecast accuracy on a monthly/quarterly/annual basis
* Maintaining competitive knowledge and focus
* In-depth knowledge of Gartner’s products and services


**Requirements:**
* 6-10 years of experience with proven consultative sales, preferably in high technology (services, software, or consultative environment), with evidence of prior success in Sales
* Strong demonstration of intellect, drive, executive presence and sales acumen
* Proven experience building excellent client relationships at C-level within large enterprise organisations
* Strong computer proficiency and presentation skills
* Professional level of Swedish
* Knowledge of the full life cycle of the sales process
* Bachelors or Masters degree – desired


**What Gartner gives you:**
* Market leading earnings potential
* Unbeatable training and growth opportunities, including an intensive 3-week sales academy for new hires
* Excellent corporate benefits
* The opportunity to sell at C-level and work with some of the brightest minds in the industry


 Your application and the data therein are received and processed by Gartner UK Ltd., which is operating as shared HR service provider for the EMEA entities of the Gartner group. Your application will be shared by Gartner UK Ltd. with those persons within the Gartner group which decide about the steps of your application.
   

  

**Who are we?**
 At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.
   

  

 Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.
   

  

 Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 21,000 associates globally who support ~14,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.
   

  

**What makes Gartner a great place to work?**
 Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.
   

  

 We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.
   

  

 Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.
   

  

 We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.
   

  

**What do we offer?**
 Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.
   

  

 In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.
   

  

 Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.
   

  

 The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.
   

  

 Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com.
   

  

 Job Requisition ID:106361
   

  

 By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the country or regional recruitment notice linked below applicable to your place of residence.
   

  

 Gartner Applicant Privacy Link: https://jobs.gartner.com/applicant-privacy-policy
   

  

**For efficient navigation through the application, please only use the back button within the application, not the back arrow within your browser.**

title: Concept Space Designer
description:
Job description:
   

  

**The Opportunity** 

 Byredo is new looking for a
 **Concept Space Designer** 
 to join our team in
 **Stockholm** 
 . This is a permanent full-time position starting in April, with an initial six-month probationary period.
 



 In this role, you will develop, design, and visualize temporary brand spaces based on creative briefs, with a particular focus on pop-ups and spatial activations. The work incorporates the development and execution of the temporary installations, and the role requires a blend of creativity, project coordination, and collaboration skills to deliver visually compelling experiences in line with the brand's aesthetics and creative direction. 
 



 Beyond temporary installations, the role may also involve designing stores, shop-in-shops, furniture, display tools, and other objects or spatial installations.
 



 You will work closely with the Head of Architecture and collaborate with the Architecture Team, as well as the Visual Merchandising, Marketing, and Graphic Design & Image teams.
 


**What you'll get to do** 
* Develop concepts for temporary installations and pop-up concepts, ensuring alignment with brand aesthetics and creative direction
* Adapt external agency concepts into executable plans for various locations while maintaining brand consistency
* Create sketches, 3D renderings, and drawings to visually present ideas and concepts derived from creative briefs
* Develop spatial layouts and floor plans, considering foot traffic, visibility, and spatial constraints
* Collaborate with the Visual Merchandising team to develop VM proposals for pop-ups and campaigns
* Oversee the end-to-end execution of pop-ups, spatial activations, and temporary installations, ensuring adherence to timelines and deliverables
* Coordinate with internal teams and external vendors to secure resources for successful implementation
* Manage budgets for each project, sourcing cost-effective materials while maintaining quality standards
* Conduct on-site visits to verify the execution of designs aligned with approved concepts and brand standards
* Respond to unforeseen challenges or requirements during installations and events, taking initiative to find creative solutions


**We'd love to meet you if you have** 
* You have a few years’ experience within the field of temporary space designs, interior design and/or architecture
* Your aesthetics fits well with the brand
* You are interested in retail design
* You are good at taking a short brief and working wide from it pushing into new ideas and solutions
* You are flexible and have a good ability to adapt to changes
* You have a good eye when it comes to details
* You enjoy working in a fast-paced environment



 Applications must be written in English and should contain
 **resume** 
 ,
 **personal letter** 
 and
 **portfolio** 
 .

title: Fintech Co-Founder / CEO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.
 


  


 We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.
 


  


 Our offer:
 


  

  
* A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to
 **€500k** 
 in funding.
  
* **1:1 sparring with unicorn founders** 
 on a weekly basis
  
* **Community** 
 : Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors
  
* **Team building** 
 : Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)
  
* **Distribution** 
 : Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine
  
* **Funding support** 
 for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)
  


  


 One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.
 


  

 Tasks
   

  
* You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Fintech
  
* You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management
  
* You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs
  
* You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding
  
* You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively
  


  

 Requirements
   

  
* You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate
  
* You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues
  
* You have excellent communication skills in the English language
  


  


 Join us and build a €1B+ company with us!

title: Principal Consultant - ServiceNow Program Lead - Stockholm, Sweden
description:
**Role** 
 – ServiceNow Program Lead
   

  

**Technology** 
 – ServiceNow, ITSM, Service Management
   

  

**Location** 
 – Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

**Business Unit** 
 – CISNGE
   

  

**Travel Expectations**
