title: (Senior) IT Consultant - Principal Investors and Private Equity | BCG Platinion
description:
Who We Are
   

  

 Do you want to turn the challenges of digitalization into the solutions of the future? Join BCG Platinion in tackling the core IT issues of leading global companies. Deploy customized platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizations to leverage the digital potential of our customers and drive true innovation, with state-of-the-art tools such as cloud computing, AI, blockchain, microservices, and containerization. Add your individual strengths to our unique team, always there to support you. Together with Boston Consulting Group and our clients, we turn digital visions into reality.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 More so than ever, technology is driving business growth and must therefore be a top consideration in M&A decision making. Assessing technological capabilities in the due diligence phase requires knowledge across IT architecture, enterprise systems and cybersecurity – we are looking for passionate talent! As a (Senior) IT Consultant for our PIPE (Principal Investors & Private Equity) practice area, you will be focused on helping the world’s largest private equity firms address their strategic technology challenges, whether they are buying or selling a business. We look for new colleagues in our offices in Stockholm, Helsinki, Oslo, and Copenhagen!
   

  

 What You'll Bring
   

  

**You can help us make a difference if you**
 ▪ bring multiple years of experience in IT strategy and consulting, professional software development or IT project management in a tech related corporate development or start-up
   

  

 ▪ gathered basic understanding of technology financials and how it impacts deal value
   

  

 ▪ are familiar with working within a Merger and Acquisition (M&A) environment, with a focus upon technology due diligence, technology carve-out, technology PMI or PE-related value creation initiative with a portfolio company
   

  

 ▪ are a strategic thinker who likes to work creatively and analytically in an environment focused on exceptional problem-solving
   

  

 ▪ bring stellar analytical and conceptual skills, strong customer focus and mental agility with a result-orientation
   

  

 ▪ obtained a university degree with above average academic performance in a STEM related field or business management
   

  

 Who You'll Work With
   

  

**Shape the world of tomorrow**
 ▪ You will drive groundbreaking projects by using agile methods and modern approaches within the scope of strategic IT implementation for clients and by actively steering the relevant modules
   

  

 ▪ You will smooth the path for tailored, future-oriented customer journeys based on precise analysis of the situation and with current market developments and trends, such as machine learning, in mind
   

  

 ▪ You will design and optimize unique IT architectures within the framework of enterprise solution requirements, modern custom software developments, and sustainable digital solutions, including cyber resilience
   

  

 Additional info
   

  

**How You Can Grow With Us**
 We guarantee constant development incentives in an open and rewarding work environment! Together with a dedicated team of top talents, you get to work on new challenges every day. Individual training offers and a culture of open feedback will provide the framework needed to explore and perfect personal strengths. Our modern structures allow focusing on the essence of our work - with easy travel booking, an excellent back-office support and comprehensive Knowledge Center. With our regular office Fridays and legendary events, we cultivate a strong and close team network beyond our case work.
   

  

*BCG Platinion is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable.*
*#BCGPlatinion*
 Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
   

  

 BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

title: Systemspecialist
description:
**Systemspecialist till Svenska Turistföreningen (STF)** 




 STF befinner sig på en utvecklingsresa där vi stärker vårt arbetssätt inom systemförvaltning och verksamhetsutveckling. Nu söker vi en systemspecialist som vill vara med och forma strukturen för våra mest verksamhetsnära system.
 



  





 Vi arbetar med tydliga systemområden, planeringsperioder och gemensam prioritering och lägger stor vikt vid struktur, samverkan och långsiktighet.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som systemspecialist ansvarar du för IT-nära förvaltning och utveckling inom ett eller flera systemområden, exempelvis CRM och ekonomi.
 



 Du har en central roll i att etablera och driva vårt arbetssätt framåt.
 



  





 Det innebär att du:
 


* för dialog med verksamheten kring behov, prioriteringar och genomförande
* driver självständigt förändringar enligt överenskommen struktur och planering
* tydliggör ansvarsfördelning mellan verksamhet och systemförvaltning
* ställer krav på leverantörer och samordnar externa kontakter
* bidrar till planering, uppföljning och överblick inom systemområdet
* dokumenterar och utvecklar systembeskrivningar och integrationsöversikter



  





 Rollen innebär ett nära samarbete med dina kollegor inom systemförvaltning samt med olika delar av verksamheten. Du arbetar självständigt inom ditt område men är en del av ett team som gemensamt utvecklar våra arbetssätt.
 



  





**Vi söker dig som:** 



* har erfarenhet av etablerade förvaltningsmodeller, exempelvis PM3 gärna i kombination med agila arbetssätt
* är van att omsätta verksamhetsbehov till tydliga krav och lösningsförslag
* trivs i dialogen med verksamheten och är trygg i att både utmana och sätta ramar
* har erfarenhet av planering och prioritering inom IT-nära utveckling
* har god förståelse för systemarkitektur och integrationsflöden
* har relevant högskoleutbildning eller motsvarande erfarenhet
* kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska



  





 Meriterande är erfarenhet av CRM- och/eller ekonomisystem samt från arbete i medlemsorganisation.
 



  





**Är det här du?** 




 Du är strukturerad, analytisk och självgående. Du trivs i en roll där tydlighet och långsiktighet är viktigt och där du får kombinera operativt arbete med ett mer strategiskt perspektiv.
 



 Då rollen har många kontaktytor är det viktigt att du är ödmjuk, prestigelös och nyfiken på att utforska verksamhetsbehov men även tydliggöra ramar så att utveckling sker strukturerat och hållbart.
 



  





**Vad erbjuder vi?** 



* Ett arbete i en organisation där värderingar är viktiga och där vårt gemensamma mål är att få fler att upptäcka Sverige. Våra värdeord omtänksam, nyfiken, äkta, professionell och engagerad genomsyrar hela vår organisation och arbetssätt.
* Härliga kollegor och ett trevligt kontor på Södermalm i Stockholm, nära bra kommunikationer.
* Möjlighet till distansarbete två dagar i veckan.
* Bra villkor och kollektivavtal.



  





  





**Intresserad av att ansöka eller få veta mer?** 




 I denna rekrytering samarbetar STF med Computer Sweden Recruitment. Vid intresse eller frågor kontakta Maria Widegren, Rekryteringskonsult på 073-423 15 50, maria.widegren@csrecruitment.se.
 



  





 Urval och intervjuer kommer att ske löpande, tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag. Vi vill ha din ansökan så snart som möjligt, senast den 10 april 2026. För att ansöka, vänligen skicka in ditt CV eller ansök med Linkedin.
 



  





 Fackligt ombud för Unionen är Maja Hök, maja.hok@stfturist.se
 



  





*Svenska turistföreningen (STF) är en ideell medlemsförening som arbetar för att alla ska ha tillgång till Sveriges natur och kultur, idag och i framtiden. Det kräver bland annat bättre möjligheter att turista hållbart, bra boenden runt om i landet, att vi slår vakt om allemansrätten och inte minst inspiration och guidning till nya äventyr.* 




*Det fantastiska med Sverige är att det alltid finns mer att upptäcka, och vårt arbete tar aldrig slut. Varje år arrangerar vi tusentals kurser och aktiviteter på våra anläggningar, och i föreningens många lokalavdelningar. Vårt mål är att vara en tydlig röst för viktiga miljöfrågor. Vi värnar om den svenska miljön, och vill att den ska finnas kvar även för kommande generationer. Där vägen tar slut ska den som vill upptäcka mer kunna fortsätta framåt.*

title: Systemspecialist inom säkerhetsteknik till Swedavias flygplatser
description:
Swedavia söker fler systemspecialister som vill vara med och bidra till tryggheten och utvecklingen på våra flygplatser. Du blir en del av ett nytt team som har ansvar för att äga, utveckla och underhålla våra säkerhetslösningar, system som både skyddar människor och gör vår verksamhet möjlig. Skicka in din ansökan redan idag!
   

  

**Vad innebär rollen?**
 Som systemtekniker ansvarar du för felsökning, planerat underhåll och vidareutveckling av Swedavias teletekniska säkerhetssystem på flygplatserna, inklusive kameraövervakning och integrerade larm- och passagesystem. Du blir en del av ett växande team under uppbyggnad, där du får möjlighet att vara med och forma rutiner och arbetssätt.
   

  

 Arbetsuppgifter och ansvarsområden:
   

  

* Utföra planerat underhåll, inklusive uppdatering av applikationer och firmware
* Delta vid anläggningsprovningar
* Ansvara för felsökning och felavhjälpning
* Delta som resurs i projekt vid driftsättning av nya anläggningskomponenter (ny- och ombyggnationer)
* Säkerställa upprätthållande och uppdatering av anläggningsdata, exempelvis larmtexter och grafik
* Samarbeta med andra discipliner inom organisationen för att uppnå önskad funktionalitet
* Uppdatera och underhålla teleteknisk dokumentation
* Bidra med konsultativt stöd internt


 I tjänsten förekommer beredskap för att säkerställa systemen fungerar dygnet runt.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
 Du trivs med komplexa problem och utmaningar, och har en naturlig förmåga att hitta lösningar. Du kommunicerar effektivt på både engelska och svenska och förstår vikten av strukturerade tidsplaner för att leverera i tid. Du tar initiativ, är flexibel och samarbetar gärna med kollegor och andra aktörer. Vi ser även att du har:
   

  

* Minst fem års dokumenterad arbetslivserfarenhet inom teletekniska säkerhetssystem
* Relevant gymnasie- eller yrkeshögskoleutbildning inom IT, teknik eller el, alternativt motsvarande kompetens genom arbetslivserfarenhet
* Flerårig dokumenterad erfarenhet av att arbeta i systemen TTLabWest Sentrion Unision och Bosch VMS
* Erfarenhet inom driftsättning av säkerhetssystemens (Sentrion Unison och Bosh VMS) hårdvara samt avancerad programmering och konfiguration i tillhörande systemapplikationer
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att arbeta med avancerad systemadministration samt felsökning och felavhjälpning i komplexa systemmiljöer
* Goda kunskaper i SQL
* B-körkort, tillgång till bil samt möjlighet att ta sig till flygplatsen inom en timme vid beredskap


 Vi ser det som meriterande om du har:
   

  

* Utbildning eller certifiering inom Pacom Unison med Sentrion
* BVMS Expert Certification eller motsvarande utbildning inom Bosch VMS
* Utbildning inom IP-kamerasystem och transmissionsutrustning


**Vad erbjuder vi dig?**
 Vi erbjuder dig en omväxlande och glad arbetsplats i ett team med stora möjligheter att påverka. Visste du att på Swedavia får du bidrag till din arbetsresa om du åker kollektivt, elbil, cykel eller tillsammans med en kollega? Du har också möjlighet att semesterväxla och byta bort din semesterersättning mot lediga dagar. Du kan läsa mer om våra förmåner på Förmåner | Om Swedavia .
   

  

 Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta jan-erik.pettersson@swedavia.se.
   

  

**Sista ansökningsdag är den 24/4, urval sker löpande och annonsen kan stängas tidigare så tveka inte att söka redan idag!**
**Hur är det att arbeta på Swedavia?**
 Vårt fokus är att möjliggöra hållbart resande och uppmuntra nytänkande för att skapa en välkomnande, trygg och inkluderande arbetsgemenskap där du kan växa och göra verklig skillnad – läs mer här! Jobba hos oss | Om Swedavia
   

  

 Då flygplatser är en säkerhetsklassad verksamhet genomgår samtliga kandidater som går vidare i rekryteringsprocessen en bakgrundskontroll. För vissa tjänster även en säkerhetsprövning. Swedavia är även en drogfri arbetsplats med nolltolerans mot droger. Drogtester genomförs före anställning och sker slumpmässigt under anställningen.

title: *Business Finance - Sr Spec. (Subl.1)
description:
**Join us to deliver a smoke‑free future as a Business Planning Analyst.**
 We have chosen to do something incredible. We are totally transforming our business and building our future with one clear purpose: to deliver a smoke‑free future. Swedish Match is an affiliate of PMI and has been a leader in the industry for more than 200 years. In the Nordic region, we are 1,800 employees based in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, and Norway, working across various functions and teams to collaboratively deliver a smoke‑free Nordic future.
   

  

 Your Day‑to‑Day
   

  

 The Business Planning Team, part of the Nordic Finance function, supports Nordic Management in delivering our transformation across the five Nordic markets. As a Business Planning Analyst, you will be part of the Finance community and interact daily with almost all other functions in the company.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities Will Include**
* Supporting the translation of strategic plans into operational business and financial plans that support local market strategy
* Providing insightful analysis to convert the consumer journey into financials and integrate it into business plans
* Preparing ad hoc analyses to support decision‑making and maximize ROI
* Interacting with Business Solutions teams to define key metrics and drivers for data modeling
* Supporting the preparation of presentations and reports that provide a clear and comprehensive view of the business
* Supporting pricing analysis and providing recommendations to the management team
* Understanding the impact of regulatory measures on business performance
* Managing local data governance to ensure alignment with company standards


 Who You Are
   

  

 Our Business Planning team works closely with many departments, so we are looking for someone with a collaborative nature and a curious mindset. You value diversity, interact easily across cultures, and embrace inclusiveness. These attributes, combined with strong analytical skills and attention to detail, will enable success in this role.
   

  

**The Ideal Candidate Will Have**
* A degree in Finance, Business Management, Economics, or Industrial Engineering
* A minimum of 2 years of experience in Finance, Business Planning, or Strategy within international companies, preferably FMCG or consumer businesses
* Fluency in spoken and written English with strong report‑writing skills
* Strong storytelling ability, influencing decisions through clear and engaging communication
* Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office, especially Excel and PowerPoint
* Experience with Power BI is an advantage
* A strong understanding of income statements from revenue through profit
* Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and logical thinking


 All candidates must have the legal right to work in Sweden.
   

  

 What We Offer
   

  

 Our Success Depends On Dedicated Employees Who Come To Work Every Day With a Sense Of Purpose And Appetite For Progress. At Swedish Match, You Can
   

  

* Shape your future and ours, with empowerment to take risks, experiment, and explore
* Be part of an inclusive and diverse culture where every contribution is respected
* Develop your skills within a global business offering extensive career opportunities
* Enjoy flexibility through a hybrid working model, balancing office and remote work based on business needs
* Take pride in supporting our promise to society to deliver a smoke‑free future


 #SWMA
   

  

**24440**

title: Myndighetshandläggare till enheten för arbetsrätt och arbetsmiljö
description:
Enheten för arbetsrätt och arbetsmiljö, Arbetsmarknadsdepartementet
   

  

 Är du en engagerad person som vill arbeta med övergripande styrning av myndigheter? Är du bra på att se helheten och snabbt sätta dig in i komplexa uppgifter? Som myndighetshandläggare hos oss får du axla en variationsrik roll i händelsernas centrum.
   

  

 Din arbetsdag. Sveriges morgondag.
   

  

 Som myndighetshandläggare ansvarar du för styrningen av myndigheter inom enhetens ansvarsområde. Det betyder arbete med budget, prognoser och regleringsbrev samt analyser av myndigheters verksamhet, resursbehov och resultat. I myndighetsstyrningen ingår även verksamhetsutveckling och samordning av processer inom myndigheternas uppdrag. Det förutsätts att du på plats stöttar övriga medarbetare på enheten i ditt kompetensområde samt att vid behov ersätta budgetsamordnaren. Arbetsuppgifterna kan även innebära att handlägga övriga frågor inom enheten. Arbetet präglas stundtals av högt tempo.
   

  

 Läs mer om vår verksamhet på www.regeringen.se.
   

  

 Din bakgrund
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har en relevant akademisk utbildning och flerårig erfarenhet av arbete med styrningsfrågor i offentlig verksamhet. Du har även arbetat med för tjänsten relevanta IT‑system.Meriterande är erfarenhet av budget- och myndighetsstyrning inom Regeringskansliet, kunskap om enhetens verksamhetsområden samt erfarenhet av arbete i IT‑systemet Hermes.
   

  

 Du behöver kunna uttrycka dig väl i tal och skrift på svenska.
   

  

 Dina egenskaper
   

  

 Regeringskansliets medarbetarkriterier är helhet, utveckling, resultat. Alla som arbetar i Regeringskansliet behöver ha helhetssyn och samarbeta för verksamhetens bästa. Du förväntas bidra med ditt perspektiv och din kompetens samt vara öppen för andras. Du kommer med idéer och nya angreppssätt för att förbättra och utveckla verksamheten och din egen kompetens. Du är inriktad på resultat i ditt agerande och anpassar dig när behov och inriktning ändras. Vi ser även att du har en förmåga att skapa förtroendefullt samarbete på alla nivåer både internt och externt samt att du värdesätter goda relationer. Vi ser även att du har en pedagogisk förmåga. Vi fäster stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Läs mer på: Medarbetarskap och ledarskap i Regeringskansliet - Regeringen.se
   

  

 Övrigt
   

  

 Detta är en tidsbegränsad anställning på ca 12 månader. Tillträde omgående eller efter överenskommelse. Befattningen är placerad i säkerhetsklass och en säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll kommer att genomföras innan beslut om anställning fattas. Du måste vara svensk medborgare, men får gärna ha utländsk bakgrund.
   

  

 Är du intresserad?
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om jobbet och vad vi erbjuder, kontakta enhetschef Anna Maria Sondell. Du är också välkommen att kontakta Rekryteringsspecialist Siri Hågefalk. Fackliga kontaktpersoner är Lina Konradsen för Saco och Åsa Bergqvist för ST. Du når dem via Regeringskansliets växel, 08-405 10 00. Vi tar gärna emot samtal från dig som är intresserad av jobbet men vi tackar nej till dig som säljer annonser och rekryteringstjänster.
   

  

 Välkommen att registrera din ansökan i vårt rekryteringsverktyg
 **senast den 10:e april 2026.**

title: Reverse Logistics & Service Specialist
description:
**We are Aarke** 




 Aarke is a Swedish design company dedicated to creating premium home essentials that elevate the everyday for people around the world. Since our founding in 2013, we’ve been on a mission to redefine the home appliances industry by merging Scandinavian design, exceptional quality, and top-tier materials - areas often overlooked in this space.
 



  





 Our almost iconic flagship product, the Aarke Carbonator, has garnered a loyal global following, with customers in more than 30 countries. Through thoughtful craftsmanship and attention to detail, Aarke continues to expand into new product categories, setting a new standard for modern home appliances. Alongside new products, we are equally focused on growing and expanding into new markets, ensuring that Aarke is present wherever our customers are.
 



  





 We are looking for a Reverse Logistics & Service Specialist to join our Operations team, responsible for managing products after sale, including returns, service, and repair.
 



  





  





**Role Overview** 




 The Reverse Logistics & Service Specialist is responsible for how Aarke manages products after sale, including returns, service, repair, refurbishment, and reintegration into circulation.
 



 Reporting directly to the COO, this role will establish and operate Aarke’s reverse logistics and service function with a primary focus on
 **product quality, circularity, and sustainability** 
 . The position combines operational responsibility with process development and requires close collaboration with Product Development, Quality, Supply Chain, Customer Support, and external partners.
 



  





 The role involves taking clear ownership of the defined area with a high degree of independence, building structure where processes, data, or ownership are unclear. It combines hands-on operational involvement with continuous improvement and a collaborative, cross-functional way of working. The role also includes raising concerns when product quality or sustainability is impacted and making well-considered decisions with a long-term perspective.
 



  





  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Manage end-to-end reverse logistics and service processes.
* Define and operate product triage and recovery, increasing the proportion of products returned to circulation as A-stock or B-stock in line with established quality standards.
* Develop and maintain service and refurbishment processes that are reliable and scalable.
* Analyse service and return data to identify recurring issues and support improvements in product design, sourcing, and usage guidance.
* Coordinate internal teams and external partners to ensure consistent execution and accountability.
* Establish standards for repairability, refurbishment, and circular product flows.



  





  





**Background & Qualifications** 



* Relevant higher education in engineering, operations, supply chain management, industrial management, or a related field.
* Experience within service operations, reverse logistics, quality, or after-sales processes for physical consumer products.
* Knowledge of Lean, Six Sigma, or other structured continuous improvement methodologies.
* Strong analytical capability and confidence working with operational systems, data, and performance metrics.
* Experience working with external partners and suppliers.



  





  





**Why join Aarke?** 




 By joining Aarke, you will become part of a talented and passionate team that is revolutionizing the home appliance industry. We offer:
 


* **Exciting growth opportunities:** 
 Aarke is expanding rapidly, providing ample opportunities for career advancement and professional development.
* **International work environment:** 
 Collaborate with colleagues from very diverse backgrounds and cultures.
* **Modern workspaces:** 
 Enjoy our brand new Stockholm office in Vasastan area, designed to inspire creativity and collaboration. We have frequent gatherings for breakfast and after work.



  





  





**How to apply?** 




 If you are ready to take on this exciting opportunity and join our team, please submit your resume and cover letter via our website. If you have any questions about the role, please contact our Chief People Officer, Anna Korling, at anna@aarke.com.
 



  





 Interviews are conducted on an ongoing basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
 



  





 We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Senior Supplier Quality Analyst
description:
**Bring more to life.**
 Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology?
   

  

 At Cepheid, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.
   

  

 You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.
   

  

 At Cepheid, we are passionate about improving health care through fast, accurate, molecular diagnostic systems and tests. As a member of our team, you’ll get to make an immediate, measurable impact on a global scale, within an environment that fosters career growth and development. Our mission drives us to develop groundbreaking solutions for the world’s most complex health challenges. Together, we bring MORE change to the world.
   

  

 Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.
   

  

 The
 **Senior Supplier Quality Analyst** 
 is responsible for managing a broad range of supplier quality elements in terms of quality and compliance with best-in-class metric reporting and process enhancements.
   

  

 This position reports to the Supplier Quality Manager and is part of the Quality department located in Solna, Sweden and will be an on-site role.
   

  

**In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To**
* Drive Supplier Quality Processes and Procedures improvements Quality team, as well as maintenance, to applicable standards and requirements
* Participate actively or lead Quality Plans/CAPA as needed
* Ensure department audit readiness and provide front/backroom support as needed
* Determine Supplier Quality data and records management and future strategies
* Drive adherence and metric reviews as well as enhancements in Approved Supplier List, Supplier Corrective Action, Score carding, Supplier Audit, and Supplier Change Notice requirements
* Conduct supplier audits


**The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include**
* Bachelor’s degree in engineering, bioscience or related discipline and several years of work experience in area of expertise combined with program/project management
* Medical regulated environment experience (21CFR820 and ISO 13485:2016)
* Proven track record with supplier quality elements of data management and supplier records structure, process and procedure enhancements, AGILE, SAP, Smartsolve or equivalent eQMS, network drive structures, metrics, and review strategies
* Demonstrated leadership skills and the ability to influence others without direct authority as well as experience representing the function during quality system audits (internal & external)
* Demonstrated superior communication skills, both oral and written as well as management presentation experience
* Fluency in English


**Travel, Motor Vehicle Record & Physical/Environment Requirements**
* Ability to travel one to three times per year in Europe, overnight stay mainly.


**It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:**
* Harmonization and/or implementation of processes and metrics on a global scale to help enable standardization and best practice
* Project Management
* Fluency in Swedish


 Cepheid, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job.
   

  

 Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.
   

  

 For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

title: Apex Operations Manager
description:
**Company Description**
*Publicis Groupe is one of the largest communications groups in the world. We are building the agency model of the future and take a holistic approach to our clients’ challenges. By fusing together our media, creative, data and technology expertise, we are able to provide clients with integrated solutions, transformative ideas and compelling consumer experiences. We call it the Power of One.
   

  

 We are proud winner of many awards and nominations, both in Sweden and internationally. But nothing makes us prouder than to work with clients and brands such as P&G, Disney, H&M, Mondelez, Volvo, VISA, Lantmännen, LEGO, Spotify, Perrigo, Renault and many more.
   

  

 At Publicis, we put our people first. We are proud to be certified by Great Place to Work (2022, 2023, 2024, 2025) and have won the Diversity Charter Sweden Awards in the category Mental Health (2025). We have also been recognized with a Magnet Award for our engaging culture (2023). Our goal is to become the leading workplace in our industry. We are based in a fantastic office in Odenplan and offer a flexible work policy.*
**Overview**
**About APEX**
 APEX is the value-based solutions division of Publicis Media. They are a distinct and next- generation global trading entity which identifies new ways to deliver value to clients. Publicis innovative offerings via direct partnerships, principal investments and trade allow clients of all to have custom solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes.
   

  

**The Role**
 As an APEX Operations Manager, you will be responsible for the day-to-day operational delivery of APEX. You’ll act as a key point of contact for stakeholders across the agency and partner ecosystem, supporting both strategic and tactical planning as well as campaign execution.
   

  

 You will collaborate closely with client leads and biddable specialists at Publicis Media to integrate APEX into media plans, while also identifying and removing potential barriers to booking and activation. Together with other senior team members, you’ll help develop solutions that make APEX easier to book, activate, and scale.
   

  

 You’ll also work hands-on with campaign activation, optimization, follow-up, and performance analysis — ensuring high quality delivery and actionable insights.
   

  

**Why join APEX?**
 APEX is in a strong growth phase, and you’ll be joining a team where curiosity, collaboration, and initiative are highly valued. We believe in sharing insights, challenging ways of working, and continuously improving — together.
   

  

 You’ll have the opportunity to play a key role in shaping and scaling APEX, work closely with leading digital specialists and partners, influence how we develop products, processes, and ways of working, and grow your expertise in a high-impact role within Publicis Media.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Work with a broad range of Publicis Media clients.
* Support strategic and tactical media planning where APEX is integrated.
* Develop proposals and recommendations for digital media investments.
* Ensure the agency has up-to-date knowledge of the APEX product portfolio.
* Log briefed campaign activity to create visibility and estimate potential revenue.
* Log booked campaign activity to enable tracking and revenue follow-up.
* Lead and coordinate campaign execution between the agency, specialists, and external partners.
* Compile campaign results and deliver clear, actionable recommendations.
* Lead onboarding of new APEX clients.
* Monitor and compile profitability at client and campaign level.


**Qualifications**
 We are looking for someone who...
   

  

* Brings a strong, solution‑driven mindset with the ability to operate effectively in a continuously evolving environment.
* Demonstrates deep understanding of the digital media ecosystem and confidently navigates its complexity.
* Balances strategic structure with hands‑on problem solving to drive tangible outcomes.
* Thrives in fast‑paced, change‑driven environments and provides stability and direction amid ambiguity.
* Takes clear ownership of deliverables and proactively identifies opportunities to improve ways of working, performance, and impact.
* Identifies opportunities where others see challenges and translates them into actionable initiatives.
* Leads and collaborates effectively across functions, engaging multiple stakeholders to drive alignment and results.


**Additional Information**
 In addition to a welcoming culture and inclusive environment with passionate colleagues we offer you:
   

  

**Career Opportunities**
 Local and Global opportunities through the Power Of One.
   

  

**Personal and Professional Learning**
 Global AI learning platform where you can participate in +30 000 classes and connect with colleagues from all over the world.
   

  

**Flexible Ways of Working**
 We want to be an employer where work life balance is cherished. We love to spend time with our colleagues at the office but also believe in the output of your work not where the work is being done.
   

  

**Work Your World**
 For six weeks a year, the world is yours. Every employee can work anywhere on the planet where there's a Publicis office. A new city, a favorite locale, or even a different country - Go. Grow. Explore.
   

  

**Mental Health**
 The happiness and health of our people is paramount to us. We work continuously on maintaining a healthy and safe working environment through initiatives that facilitate the well-being of our employees.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Generous benefit package.
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: Senior Account Partner
description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*
 Job Category
   

  

 Customer Success
   

  

 Job Details
   

  

******About Salesforce******
 Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.
   

  

 Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.
   

  

****About Salesforce****
 Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about improving business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.
   

  

 Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.
   

  

 The Account Partner (AP) is a proven, passionate, strategic sales executive responsible for selling the full portfolio of Professional Services offerings to Salesforce’s most strategic and ambitious customers. The AP must be a strategic thinker with exceptional enterprise, commercial, and cross-functional skills, demonstrate high energy, and have strong sales experience. The AP is responsible for earning the right to be a trusted advisor to the customer, with the primary goal of helping customers generate significant business value from their Salesforce investment. You will be expected to understand and manage our customers throughout their lifecycle, driving adoption through sales activity for new and existing customers; this will include pipeline generation & solution selling to C-level customers. The primary measurement of success for this role will identifying be YoY growth in overall services bookings and revenue.
   

  

**See here for more info on Prof Services** 
 -http://www.salesforce.com/services/professional-services/overview/
   

  

****Responsibilities:****
* Exceed Professional Services Sales growth and bookings, ensure organisational health and customer satisfaction goals as a regional leader
* Utilise industry expertise and business acumen to understand a customer's motivation, business drivers, strategic goals and objectives, and desired business outcomes
* Engage customers, especially C-Level, using a consultative solution selling approach that positions Salesforce and yourself as a long-term trusted advisor relationship
* Create a compelling vision and clearly communicate our transformative solutions with the goal of generating significant success and business value from a customer's investment in the Salesforce Platform
* Develop and manage a territory plan and a personalised account plan for each customer, which aligns with their business goals. Forecast accurately and timely, build a pipeline and progress opportunities to deliver Professional Services YOY revenue growth
* Sell as a team, forming strong relationship with our License Sales organisation and regional Alliances/Partner organisations that are instrumental to success
* Be a recognised role model for collaboration, leadership and overall business results


****As An Account Partner** 
 you
 **:****
* are internally and externally recognised as business contributor, industry or product specialist.
* have high level of knowledge of emerging business / industry trends, competitive direction and product direction.
* determine the best customer success approach based on general direction from senior management.
* work on complex business issue and initiatives that require business or industry knowledge.
* act as an advisor on complex issues and initiatives, exercising discretion and latitude in reaching their goals. Leads cross functional teams and mentors others.


****Preferred Qualifications & Skills:****
* Demonstrated consultative sales experience with a proven record of selling and/or delivering professional services for a strategic consulting firm or large scale system integrator.
* Demonstrated ability to develop and maintain C-level relationships where you are recognised as a trusted advisor
* Experience growing accounts with large and complex pursuits ($M+)
* Highly collaborative and excels in a complex, matrixed environment
* Team player with strong interpersonal skills
* Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment


 Li\*Y
   

  

 Unleash Your Potential
   

  

 When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and
 *be your best* 
 , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can
 *do your best* 
 . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.
   

  

 Accommodations
   

  

 If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form.
   

  

 Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.
   

  

 Posting Statement
   

  

 Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer | Predictions Team
description:
Every parcel we deliver starts with a prediction
   

  

 At Instabee, our Predictions Team builds the forecasting engines that power one of Europe's fastest-growing logistics networks — from delivery volume predictions to real-time resource scheduling across five markets.
   

  

 We work end-to-end. That means you won't hand off a notebook and call it done. You'll own the full lifecycle: data discovery, model development, production deployment, and ongoing monitoring. If that sounds like the kind of ownership you've been looking for, keep reading.
   

  

**About Your Future Team**
 The Predictions Team sits at the core of how Instabee operates. We are a tight-knit group of engineers with a direct line to the decisions that shape our entire logistics network — from how routes are planned to how we staff our terminals weeks in advance.
   

  

 We take end-to-end ownership seriously. We architect, deploy, and monitor production-grade systems that run at scale across five markets. Our forecasts aren't just nice-to-have insights; they are the inputs that drive real operational decisions every single day.
   

  

 But great models alone aren't enough. We work closely with stakeholders across operations, commercial, and leadership — translating complex analytical outputs into clear, actionable insights that non-technical audiences can act on with confidence. Understanding the business behind the data is just as important to us as the engineering that powers it. The best solutions come from engineers who can sit in a room with a decision-maker, understand what they actually need, and build something that delivers exactly that.
   

  

 We move fast, we care about craft, and we believe the best solutions come from engineers who understand both the data and the business. If you want to work on genuinely hard problems with people who take quality seriously — you'll fit right in.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Design & Develop — Build and refine time-series and ML models that predict delivery volumes with high precision
* Productionize — Take full ownership of deployment via robust CI/CD pipelines into production environments
* Architect Infrastructure — Use containerization and orchestration tools to ensure prediction services scale with the business
* Data Engineering — Collaborate on data collection and preprocessing logic
* Monitor & Optimize — Track performance drift and maintain system health across deployed models


 Your Tech Stack
   

  

**Need To Have**
* Strong skills in Python and SQL
* Strong command of time-series forecasting, neural networks, and tree-based models — built, shipped, and monitored in production
* Hands-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes — containerizing and orchestrating ML workloads in production
* Proficiency in GCP (or similar) and Terraform — managing cloud infrastructure as code


**Nice To Have**
* Practical experience with stream processing (PubSub or similar) — building pipelines that power real-time predictions
* Background in logistics or supply chain — you understand the operational reality behind the data
* Hands-on experience with Python libraries such as Polars, Prophet, NeuralProphet, or PyTorch — applied to real forecasting challenges
* Experience with orchestration tools such as Argo Workflows — scheduling and managing ML pipelines at scale


 🌟 What You'll Get
   

  

* 🌇 Sky-high office with a 360° view over Stockholm
* ⏰ Flexible hybrid setup
* 💡 Hack Days twice a year
* 🐶 Dog-friendly workplace
* 🥐 Wednesday Tech Breakfasts
* 👾 Regular Tech Demos to stay close to what's coming next
* 💻 Paid licenses for advanced dev tools and AI coding agents
* 👶 Six months parental pay top-up
* 🎂 Your birthday off — every year


 Diversity and inclusion are very important to us and we are committed to creating an inclusive work environment. We want to be a workplace where everyone’s perspectives and opinions are valued, and we are dedicated to creating opportunities for all employees to thrive and reach their full potential.
   

  

 Our Recruitment Process
   

  

* 👋 TA Interview — A first conversation to get to know each other
* 🧠 Hiring Manager Interview — A deeper dive into your experience and ways of working
* 🔬 Technical Case — A chance to show us how you think and solve problems
* 🤝 Meet & Greet — A conversation with your future team
* 📋 Reference Check — We'll speak to a couple of people who know your work
* 🔍 Background Control — A standard check for all hires in Sweden
* 🎉 Contract Signing — Welcome to Instabee!


*This recruitment process consists of background checks via Verifiera as a review of previous public criminal records. The result is public information that is handled confidentially. This is mandatory for all recruitment processes at Instabee in Sweden.*
 Sound like your kind of challenge? We'd love to hear from you.

title: SAP CFIN Consultant
description:
**Job Title: SAP CFIN Consultant** 




**Job Location: Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Job Type: Permanent** 




  





**Key Responsibilities:** 




  




* **CFIN Solution Design & Configuration:** 
 Design and configure SAP S/4HANA Central Finance, including source system mapping, financial structure harmonization, and posting logic.
* **Real-Time Replication & SLT:** 
 Configure and support SLT-based replication of FI documents from source systems to S/4HANA Central Finance.

 Drive harmonization of chart of accounts, cost centers, profit centers, and controlling objects across source systems.

 Collaborate with finance, controlling, audit, and IT stakeholders to resolve issues and support business decisions.

 Ensure assigned deliverables are completed on time, meeting quality standards and program governance requirements.
* **Documentation & Hypercare:** 
 Prepare configuration documents, functional specifications, test scripts, and provide post go-live hypercare support.

title: Commercial Business Controller
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a commercially driven
 **Business Controller** 
 in our Stockholm office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

 The role
   

  

 We are seeking an experienced and commercial Business Controller to join our finance team, focusing on our Allente business. As a Business Controller, you will play a crucial role in financial planning, analysis, and decision-making. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating with cross-functional teams, this opportunity is for you!
   

  

* Strategic partnership: close collaboration with the commercial leadership organisation, driving the financial agenda and providing actionable and insights and support business excellence.
* Financial Analysis: Analyse financial data, identify trends, and provide insights to support business growth.
* Budgeting and Forecasting: Collaborate with department heads to create budgets, forecasts, and financial plans.
* Performance Metrics: Develop and track KPIs to measure business performance and identify areas for improvement.
* Cost Control: Monitor expenses, identify cost-saving opportunities, and implement efficient processes.
* Management Reporting: Prepare accurate and timely reports for management and stakeholders.
* Ad Hoc Projects: Contribute to strategic projects and initiatives as needed.


 What we’re looking for
   

  

**Role**
 We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:
   

  

* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field.
* 4-7 years of experience as a Business Controller or similar role.
* Proficiency in financial modelling, Excel, and BI systems.
* Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.


**It’s a Bonus If You Have**
* Experience of working in the entertainment industry or Telco industry.


 What we offer you
   

  

**We’ve got you covered! 30 days** 
 of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
   

  

**Your wellbeing matters.** 
 We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
   

  

**A safe space to grow and up-skill.** 
 Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
   

  

**Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do** 
 . So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including series & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.
   

  

 Hit play today
   

  

 If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
   

  

 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: Retail Business Development Manager - Central Europe
description:
Aarke is a Swedish design company offering a range of premium home essentials engineered to elevate everyday routines. Founded in 2013, we are on a mission to re(de)fine the home appliances industry. Our flagship product, the Aarke Carbonator, has gained a cult following worldwide, with customers in more than 30 countries. In a category often overlooked by the design community, Aarke aims to combine enduring quality, premium materials, and a distinctive design approach.
 



  





 Aarke is on an inspiring growth journey, with our employees as our most valuable asset. As part of this journey, our Wholesale team seeks a commercially sharp and hands-on Retail Business Development Manager to drive growth in Central Europe.
 



  





 You can read more about Aarke @ aarke.com.
 



  





  





**The Role** 




 As Retail Business Development Manger for Central Europe, you are responsible for driving sustainable and profitable growth across the region. You will manage distributors and key retail accounts, ensure strong execution in the market, and continuously identify opportunities to improve performance.
 



  





 This is a hands-on commercial role where you are expected to stay close to the market, ensuring strong retail execution and brand positioning while building structured plans and forecasts to support long-term growth. You combine commercial drive with analytical rigor and use data to prioritize, challenge and improve results.
 



  





 Frequent travel across Central Europe is required.
 



  





 You will report to Head of Wholesale and Retail.
 



  





  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Own regional sales targets, forecasting, and profitability, ensuring sustainable growth and financial performance.
* Develop customer-level demand forecasts to support supply planning and inventory management.
* Manage and develop distributor and key retail partner relationships to drive long-term growth.
* Expand and optimize regional distribution, ensuring strong geographic coverage and a premium customer experience.
* Ensure channel mix, portfolio positioning, and retail segmentation align with Aarke’s strategic priorities.
* Track and optimize commercial performance through structured monitoring of sell-in, sell-out, sell-through, stock levels, campaign ROI, and portfolio mix.
* Build annual and joint business plans with partners to drive aligned growth initiatives.
* Lead regional go-to-market execution for product launches in line with global strategy.
* Identify and drive market growth opportunities through data-driven analysis and structured market follow-up.
* Collaborate closely with internal functions including Brand, Product Development, Supply Chain, and Finance to ensure aligned execution and operational excellence.



  





  





**Who You Are** 




 You bring a modern commercial mindset and thrive in a dynamic, international scale-up environment where structures evolve and adaptability is key. You are curious about markets and consumer behavior and enjoy being close to execution — not just strategy. You are as comfortable analyzing sales data as you are meeting buyers and distributors and driving performance discussions based on facts and insights. You take ownership, work in a structured way, and continuously look for ways to improve how things are done.
 



  





  





**We believe you:** 



* Have experience from Business Development/ Sales within FMCG, premium home appliances or similar fast-moving, consumer-driven industries
* Are used to working with clear KPIs, forecasting and performance tracking
* Combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution
* Are confident using data and insights to drive commercial decisions
* Thrive in an environment where pace is high and change is constant



  





  





**Profile** 



* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Economics or similar (Master’s degree is considered a plus)
* 5+ years of experience in commercial roles within consumer-driven industries
* Experience from management consulting or similarly analytical environments is desirable.
* Proven experience managing regional sales targets and forecasts
* Strong analytical skills and high commercial acumen
* Experience working with distributors and established retail accounts
* Strong negotiation and stakeholder management skills
* Fluency in written and spoken English and German; proficiency in any additional Central European language is a plus.



  





  





**What we can offer you** 




 At Aarke you get to work with consumer products that people can relate to and are amongst the most loved in their respective category. We are constantly looking to improve our way of working and you are welcome and expected to support and influence our growth journey. At Aarke you will find a hard-working, fun, interesting, and diverse group of professionals with a range of different backgrounds.
 



  





 Our headquarters is in central Stockholm, Vasastan.
 



  





  





**What now?** 




 Please submit your application through career.aarke.com/jobs, we will be reviewing applicants continuously. If you have questions about the position, please contact Chief People Officer, Anna Korling at anna@aarke.com
 



  





  





**Diversity** 




 At Aarke, we do not discriminate based on gender, race, sexual orientation, creed, or background. Constant change, learning, and evolution are core tenets of our brand mission, and are equally as relevant when it comes to diversity and social awareness.

title: Technical Sales Specialist - HVAC
description:
**Job Description**
**Brief Job Description:**
 Plan, perform and manage the application of standard engineering methods and techniques on multidisciplinary controls projects under the direction of the Product Managers.
   

  

**Responsibilities and Measurement Criteria with Time investment Needed on Each:**
* Plan, perform and manage the application of standard engineering methods and techniques on multidisciplinary controls projects under the direction of the Product Managers.
* Assist in development of standard and custom software, defining new and advanced features required and manage schedule of each projects.
* Assist with specification, design and layout of control systems for Asia Thermal Management Products.
* Assist and review Thermal Management control systems including the design, controls panels and electrical schematics.
* Ability to read, interpret, and produce engineering documents that may include: Control Drawings, Sequence of Operations, Interface requirements, and Functional Specifications
* Develop technical documents to support local and field team in Asia on various areas of Control & Monitoring application as necessary.
* Provide Control & Monitoring training, start up and commissioning guide documentation.
* Connect unit control via the Internet, modems, and other communication formats for remote troubleshooting and finding root cause.
* Coordinate Thermal Control system’s ethernet and IT requirements with internal IT operations.
* Provide support on interface issue on multiple communication protocols such as SNMP, BACnet, and Modbus and having experience using troubleshooting tools/software.
* Assist marketing in preparing Asia Thermal Control and Monitoring related materials.
* Travel to countries in the region to provide support in the field when necessary (after International Travel lifted)


**Qualifications**
**Required/ Minimum Qualifications:**
* Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Computer Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, HVAC Technology or Visual Basic or C++ with Database programming experience.
* Minimum 3 years’ experience with HVAC Control System/Building Automation System & Industrial Control Programmable Logic Controllers (PLC)


**Additional / Preferred Qualifications**
* Understand electrical systems, appropriate codes, and mechanical standards
* Ability to install controls systems when required
* Strong computer skills for networking control systems and software.
* Knowledge of BAS/BMS systems, ALC, Andover, Tridium, Delta Controls or others.
* Basic to advanced AutoCAD proficiency, Microsoft office suite, and basic windows environment
* Must speak fluent Mandarin and English (Note: Needs to communicate frequently with R&D team or other departments in China organization)
* Working knowledge of 3 years in built up mechanical systems such as HVAC Control and VAV air systems
* Ability to diagnose mechanical systems and controls
* Attend customer meeting presenting control solution and provide technical clarification
* Hands-on experience with control engineering and programming
* Field experience in installation, testing & PID tuning of HVAC equipment.

**Time Travel Needed:**
 The successful candidate will embrace Vertiv’s Core Principals & Behaviors to help execute our Strategic Priorities.

 Safety. Integrity. Respect. Teamwork. Diversity & Inclusion.

* Customer Focus
* Operational Excellence
* High-Performance Culture
* Innovation
* Financial Strength


***OUR BEHAVIORS***
* Own It
* Act With Urgency
* Foster a Customer-First Mindset
* Think Big and Execute
* Lead by Example
* Drive Continuous Improvement
* Learn and Seek Out Development

With $5 billion in sales, a strong customer base and global reach in nearly 70 countries, our move to establish a standalone business enables us to deliver greater value to our customers and create new opportunities for our people.*

title: HR-controller till Försvarshögskolan
description:
Arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Som HR-controller kommer du ha en central roll och samarbeta tätt med HR-chef, HR-partners, lönespecialister, ekonomiavdelning och IT. Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter är att driva lönebildnings- och lönekartläggningsprocesserna samt ta fram och analysera statistik och data som beslutsstöd för Försvarshögskolan. Du stödjer verksamheten med analyser och rapporter kring alla former av personalekonomi. I rollen ingår även att arbeta med projekt och ständiga förbättringar inom HR-området, där du bidrar till att utveckla och effektivisera HR-processer och system.
   

  

 Vem söker vi?
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som Har**
* Erfarenhet som lönespecialist i systemet Primula
* Erfarenhet av att ha drivit lönebildnings- och lönekartläggningsfrågor
* Erfarenhet av att aktivt ha stöttat i lönerevisionsarbete
* Utvecklat administrativa processer och rutiner
* God kunskap i systemet Analyskraft
* Intresse av teknikutveckling


**Meriterande Är**
* Erfarenhet av implementering av HR-system.


 Som person är du självgående och proaktiv. Vidare har du en god förmåga att prioritera och strukturera ditt arbete samt samarbeta och kommunicera med andra inom och utanför HR-området. Du är analytisk och har hög integritet.
   

  

 För att trivas hos oss behöver du vara en engagerad och närvarande individ som vill bidra till Försvarshögskolans professionella utveckling, kollegiala samarbetskultur och sociala sammanhållning. Det fallar sig naturligt för dig att dela med dig av din kunskap och bidra till att skapa en givande och god arbetsmiljö.
   

  

 Om anställningen
   

  

 Anställningen är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid som inleds med en sex månaders provanställning.
   

  

 En anställning vid Försvarshögskolan kan komma att innebära en säkerhetsprövning för placering i säkerhetsklass vilket innebär registerkontroll och i vissa fall säkerhetsintervju hos Säkerhetspolisen.
   

  

 Anställningen kan också komma att innefatta krigsplacering. Om du redan är krigsplacerad i din nuvarande anställning på en myndighet och din kompetens behövs i Försvarshögskolans krigsorganisation sker det en förhandling mellan berörd myndighet och Försvarshögskolan om var du ska vara placerad.
   

  

 Tillträde
   

  

 Enligt överenskommelse.
   

  

 Lön och förmåner
   

  

 Försvarshögskolan tillämpar individuell lönesättning.
   

  

 Förmåner för anställda vid Försvarshögskolan - FHS/Förmåner/anställd
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Ansökan ska innehålla ett CV samt ett kort personligt brev och skickas in via Försvarshögskolans rekryteringssystem genom att klicka på länken ”Logga in och sök jobbet” på annonssidan. Märk din ansökan med dnr HR 36/2026. Din ansökan ska vara tillhanda senast 2026-03-25.
   

  

 Komplett ansökan ska vara Försvarshögskolan tillhanda senast sista ansökningsdagen vid midnatt, CET/CEST (Central European Time/Central European Summer Time).
   

  

 I de fall det inte råder en jämn könsbalans kan Försvarshögskolan vid lika eller i stort sätt lika kvalifikationer hos de sökande anställa den av underrepresenterat kön.
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 25 mars 2026!

title: Intelligent Automation – Senior Business Analyst, Pega
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 CGI's practice inom intelligent automation växer och vi letar efter seniora BAs med stark domänkunskap inom bank (gärna lending) som kommer ingå i vår Financial Services enhet i Stockholm. Vi är många som är specialiserade på Pega plattformen och vi utvärderar kontinuerligt marknaden.
   

  

 Kom och bli en del av ett växande team av experter med olika bakgrunder och branschkunskap. Om du är en senior konsult som vill fortsätta karriären i ett stort globalt bolag med trygghet och en stark lokal förankring är vi rätt för dig.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Arbetet på CGI omfattas av en dynamisk arbetsmiljö och en spännande vardag. Vi erbjuder dig möjligheten att utvecklas som konsult både i lokala kundleveranser och i större globala åtaganden. Det finns goda möjligheter till individuell kompetensutveckling, vilket gör att du kontinuerligt kan bidra värdefullt både till dina egna ansvarsområden och till projektets helhet.
   

  

 Du kan komma att arbeta i kortare fokuserade projekt eller i större, långsiktiga åtaganden. Vi deltar i och driver projekt både på plats hos kund och från våra lokala kontor. Vi arbetar både individuellt och i team tillsammans med kollegor. Våra uppdrag genomförs med stöd av etablerade metoder och modeller baserade på branschstandarder.
   

  

 Vi bygger framtidens ledare - och vi har roligt på vägen!
   

  

 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 Vi söker seniora experter med fokus på intelligent automation och erfarenhet inom bank, gärna inom Lending. Pega Business Architect certifiering är inte ett krav men vi förväntar oss att du vill lära dig plattformen och ta nödvändiga certifikat. Vi premierar konsulter som redan bor och arbetar i Stockholm då det är här våra kunder finns.
   

  

 För denna roll prioriterar vi kandidater som behärskar svenska flytande i tal och skrift, samt har en akademisk examen och relevant arbetslivserfarenhet. Vi förväntar oss att du har minst 8+ års erfarenhet som business analyst eller business architect. Du trivs i rollen mellan verksamhet och IT och känner dig trygg i att driva kravarbete i både stora och små organisationer.
   

  

 Du deltar och driver utformningen av färdplan och riktning för att bygga framtidens betalningslösningar och/eller lånaaffären. Självklart samarbetar du med arkitekter och leveransledare för att driva strategiska lösningar i samband med pågående projektleveranser, vilket kräver stark drivkraft, diplomati och pragmatism. Du kan hantera och leverera både funktionella och icke funktionella krav.
   

  

 Du är skicklig på att planera och genomföra resultatinriktade workshops och kan både muntligt och visuellt fånga krav samt leverera ditt underlag i tid. Vi förväntar oss att du kan driva nya initiativ från start till mål utifrån ett kravperspektiv och att du är van att jobba agilt samt kunna hantera olika typer av stakeholders hos kunden.
   

  

**Vill du veta mer?**
 Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig! Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande så skicka in din ansökan redan i dag! Om du har frågor om rollen, hör gärna av dig till rekryterande ledare Carl Ahlin, Director Consulting Services/ Konsultchef, på ca.larsson@cgi.com
   

  

**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Manager Bar Fab & Test Engineering
description:
**Job Description**
**Coherent Corp.** 
 is the global leader in photonics, empowering innovators to shape the future with breakthrough technologies, from advanced materials to complete laser and optical systems.
   

  

 Our solutions impact industrial, communications, electronics, and instrumentation markets, helping customers overcome complex challenges and unlock new possibilities. Headquartered in Saxonburg, Pennsylvania, we operate worldwide, offering an international, collaborative environment where research, manufacturing, and customer-focused teams drive innovation and deliver real-world results.
   

  

**Inside Our Järfälla Site**
*Coherent Järfälla is where cutting-edge photonics meets everyday teamwork. Home to Sweden’s only full-scale indium phosphide (InP) wafer fab, we design, produce, and test some of the world’s fastest optical chips – all under one roof.*
*Our engineers don’t just design technology; they see it move from wafer to finished product, powering data centres, AI infrastructure, and global communications. With around 300 specialists on site, strong continued growth, and ongoing investment in people and equipment, Järfälla offers real impact, collaboration, and long-term career development in a high-tech environment.*
**Location:** 
 Järfälla
 **| Onsite** 
**| Employment Type:** 
 Permanent
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The Bar Fab & Test Engineering Manager leads the engineering team responsible for Bar Fabrication and Test equipment within Coherent Järfälla Operations. This role is accountable for developing people, processes, and technologies to deliver manufacturing objectives across efficiency, yield, quality, and capacity.
   

  

 You will provide hands-on leadership to production engineering activities, drive continuous improvement initiatives, support manufacturing operations, and collaborate closely with cross-functional partners across Operations, R&D, Quality, and global Coherent sites.
   

  

**Roles & Responsibilities**
* Lead and develop the Bar Fab & Test Engineering team within Operations, building capability through coaching, performance management, and continuous improvement
* Own engineering responsibility for Bar Fabrication (cleaving, stacking, handling), Test Engineering (wafer probe, laser bar testing), and Automated Optical Inspection (AOI)
* Provide day-to-day production engineering support, including corrective actions and process optimisation
* Develop and improve manufacturing processes, technologies, and work practices to drive yield, quality, efficiency, and capacity
* Identify, lead, and implement process and equipment improvement initiatives
* Support New Product Introduction (NPI) projects to enable extended manufacturing capabilities
* Train and support operators and supervisors
* Monitor, analyse, and report manufacturing engineering metrics
* Partner with Maintenance on preventive maintenance planning and spare-parts strategy
* Collaborate across Operations, R&D, Quality, Product Engineering, and global Coherent sites
* Manage supplier interactions as requires
* Perform core people-management responsibilities, including planning, delegating, and decision-making within the assigned process area


**About You**
* Master of Science in Engineering (or equivalent)
* 5–10 years of experience in Manufacturing Engineering or similar roles
* Substantial experience in:
* Semiconductor test engineering
* Laser bar cleaving and stacking
* High-volume, high-precision manufacturing environments
* Demonstrated initiative and practical leadership capability
* Strong communication, organisational, and stakeholder-management skills
* Ability to work independently with general direction to achieve objective
* Language Requirements: Fluent in Swedish and English (written and spoken)


**CULTURE COMMITMENT**
 Ensure adherence to company’s values (ICARE) in all aspects of your position at Coherent:
   

  

**I** 
 ntegrity – Create an Environment of Trust
   

  

**C** 
 ollaboration – Innovate Through the Sharing of Ideas
   

  

**A** 
 ccountability – Own the Process and the Outcome
   

  

**R** 
 espect – Recognize the Value in Everyone
   

  

**E** 
 nthusiasm – Find a Sense of Purpose in Work
   

  

 Current employees who wish to apply should submit a cover letter detailing their interest in the position and how their skills and experience align with the requirements of the role.
   

  

 Applications for this tole close COB on
 **Friday, 20 February 2026** 
 .If you require any adjustment to the application process, please reach out to Alyxandra.Highland@coherent.com
   

  

**About Us**
 Coherent is a global leader in lasers, engineered materials and networking components. We are a vertically integrated manufacturing company that develops innovative products for diversified applications in the industrial, optical communications, military, life sciences, semiconductor equipment, and consumer markets. Coherent provides a comprehensive career development platform within an environment that challenges employees to perform at their best, while rewarding excellence and hard-work through a competitive compensation program. It's an exciting opportunity to work for a company that offers stability, longevity and growth. Come Join Us!
   

  

**Note to recruiters and employment agencies:** 
 We will not pay for unsolicited resumes from recruiters and employment agencies unless we have a signed agreement and have required assistance, in writing, for a specific opening.

title: Master's thesis: Implementation of Higher-Layer Protocols for 6G Ambient IoT
description:
Master's thesis: Implementation of Higher-Layer Protocols for 6G Ambient IoT
   

  

**Background**
 6G is expected to transform wireless connectivity by enabling extremely high data rates, ultralow latency, and the capacity to support vast numbers of Internet of Things (IoT) devices, well beyond what 5G can provide. A key pillar of this vision is Ambient 6G IoT, which aims to seamlessly integrate billions of devices into the network and unlock new applications across sectors such as smart cities, precision agriculture, environmental monitoring, and advanced healthcare. These massive deployments will also generate rich data resources that fuel AI models, digital twins, and emerging 6G services such as holographic communication and immersive extended reality experiences.
   

  

**Project Description**
 The goal of this thesis is to design and implement a prototype of selected higher-layer protocols for Ambient 6G IoT systems. The work will involve implementing parts of the protocol stack on both the device side, representing constrained IoT nodes, and on the network side, representing a base station or gateway. The implementation will make it possible to experiment with the protocols in a realistic setting and evaluate their behavior under practical constraints.
   

  

 The project will include implementing a subset of the higher-layer Ambient IoT protocols and integrating them into a working prototype. The implementation will then be used to study system characteristics such as protocol overhead, communication latency, and resource usage on constrained devices, including memory consumption. The results are expected to provide insights into the practical feasibility of these protocols and help inform future research and development in Ambient IoT systems for 6G.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 The thesis is suitable for students interested in networking, IoT systems, distributed systems, or embedded development, and who enjoy implementing and experimenting with new protocols. Programming experience in languages such as C, Python, or similar environments is beneficial.
   

  

**What We Offer**
* A chance to work on a scientifically novel project that is defining the future of Ambient IoT for 6G.
* Hands-on experience with state-of-the-art IoT and 5G frameworks.
* Access to RISE’s advanced resources, unique datasets, and test environments.
* Opportunities for professional growth and networking, including potential PhD opportunities.
* A friendly and dynamic research environment with experienced supervisors.


**Terms**
* Scope: The thesis usually comprises 15 or 30 credits (hp/ECTS).
* Start Date: Spring 2026, or by agreement.
* Location: RISE, Kista, Stockholm, with the possibility for some remote work.
* Compensation: In line with RISE guidelines for strategically important projects, a compensation of 1333 SEK/credit is offered upon completion and approval of the thesis.


**Welcome with your application!**
 Applications must be submitted through the recruitment portal.
   

  

**Last day of application:** 
 March 31st, 2026.
   

  

**For more information, please contact:**
 Professor Thiemo Voigt
   

  

 Dr. Joakim Eriksson

title: Sales Advisor 25h & 8h - Summer Substitute
description:
**Job Description**

**WHAT YOU’LL DO** 
**​**
  

 As a Sales Advisor at H&M, you’ll play a key role in creating an outstanding customer experience. You’ll welcome customers, guide them through your store, and support them in finding what they need while showcasing our products. Acting in line with our values, you’ll contribute to both your own success and the success of the company.
   

  





 You will:
   

  




* Share your fashion and product knowledge to help customers make informed choices.
* Collaborate with your team to deliver exceptional service at every step of the customer journey.
* Ensure the sales floor and back of house are well-stocked, organized, and inviting.
* Support with opening and closing of the store.
* Represent yourself and the brand positively during all customer interactions.



**WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH** 
**​**




 Our stores are the heart of our company, where customers experience our brand firsthand. As part of the dynamic Store Team, every role—from Sales Advisors, Department Managers, Store Managers and Visual Merchandisers to Assistant Store Managers, Cash Office Responsible, and Delivery Responsible in larger stores—contributes to creating an inspiring and welcoming environment. You’ll also connect with the wider Area Team, collaborating across stores to share insights and drive success together. Alongside your team, you'll play a vital role in helping customers feel confident and express their individuality with the latest trends and timeless styles. By building meaningful connections with customers in our stores, we make fashion accessible and sustainable for all.
   

  





**WHO YOU ARE** 
**​**




*We are looking for people with…* 
 ​
   

  




* Experience in customer service within fashion, retail, or similar fields.



*And people who are…* 
 ​
   

  




* Passionate about fashion and delivering an exceptional in-store experience while promoting our products and driving sales.
* Thriving in collaborative and dynamic environments.
* Communicative, creative, curious to work with store technology, with a strong eagerness to learn and develop.
* Flexible and action oriented.



**WHO WE ARE** 
**​**
